- I'm going to share with you the 12 hacks that keep me insanely productive 99% of every day these strategies will allow the top 1% to get more done in a day than most people get done in a week they're what I use despite having crippling ADHD to become a millionaire by age 27 sell three companies and build $100 million business Empire so without further explaining it these are the 12 ways to be more productive that cost nothing the first hack is to increase your cycle time a couple of years ago I was hiring a bunch of copywriters and I noticed that one of them was 10 times more productive than the other two it was interesting cuz eventually I ended up meeting up with them for a work session and I saw one copywriter type with all 10 figures using hot keys while the others just type with two fingers like fifth graders it never occurred to me that a copywriter wouldn't know how to type the person who is 10 times more productive they also never touched their Mouse they were using hot Keys the whole time which made their output be that much faster the first rule of productivity is to just get faster at the thing you're doing increase the speed of your mouse if you go in there and just fast forward the speed of your mouse it might feel weird at first but you'll eventually get used to it listening to playbacks on any type of audio put it at 1.5 2x just gets faster learning how to type taking typing classes typing.com learning the hotkeys for your email tool your productivity tool to make sure that everything is just one keyboard click away from getting done an advanced version would be automation with AI think zapier or other tools like it where you can plug into workflows and use the AI to understand what your manual ually doing for it to process it and automate the whole thing so you never have to touch that work again it could be everything from processing comments on your social media to connecting your inbox so that once a day maybe twice a day you get a summary of all your emails using AI to figure out which ones are high priority which one are low with even recommended text to reply to those emails learning how to automate and increase workflow and be productive cost you nothing but improves the cycle time which brings us to number two which is to make a stop doing list when I'm speaking at a Recovery Center or at a group home for troubled youth I always allow the kids to sit in my McLaren and often times they ask so what do you actually do and I usually respond with it's not what I do it's what I don't do most of us are doing things that take up our time and we don't understand this core principle called the 9010 principle it's 10% of your work drives 90% of your results so the key thing is to figure out what are your strengths what are you great at Double Down on those stop trying to become great at something you hate do enough so that it's not a deficit but definitely don't spend all your time trying to take something you hate doing to try to make it something great when you're good at something and go be world class at it also look at all your vices when I say it's what I don't do I don't drink I don't hang out with my friends and talk about other people I don't gamble I don't Vape there's all the stuff I don't do to create the space for me to be more productive I'm also a big fan of finding people that are good at the things I'm bad at and love to do the things I hate there's people out there that literally play at things that I consider work my bookkeeper she would rather sit there and stare at a spreadsheet and reconcile bank accounts than anything else I would hate to do that I'd rather be on the phone talking to people I'd rather be at an event presenting so just understanding there's other people out there that could buy back your time that could help you out you can trade you can ask for favors another idea is to actually make a list of all the things that drain your energy things that you did in the last 6 months that you wish you wouldn't have done make that list be clear and then ask yourself in the future when those opportunities come up I'm going to make sure I immediately say no and it's as simple as a no reviewing the last 6 months and deciding what things you did that you would never want to do and put that on the stop doing list is a good strategy which brings us to number three which is to create urgency I don't know if you can relate but I'm the kind of guy back in the day where I would wait till the last minute to work on a big project either homework for school or big presentation at my work so that I created a sense of urgency to get it done I used to tell myself some weird line about like oh that's when I'm most creative when I have pressure on myself but it turned out it made it really bad for everybody else in my life it meant I didn't prepare and it just created a lot of emotional shrapnel that didn't need to exist this is exactly how I do it today first off is I set an earlier deadline for completion I know I'm making this up in my head that's fine whatever is the due date I make the date sometimes 2 weeks before why because I just want that urgency to focus my creativity that allows me to come prepared to all these big events in my life if it's prepping for a podcast I'm going on or working on my book deadlines or whatever it is is I'd rather have the date way sooner that it's needed so that I also have the breathing room to be more creative and more inspired another strategy I love to use is to block time in my calendar usually about 90 minutes per project and what I'll do is I'll set a timer I like to use a Pomodoro Timer which is 25 minutes of focused work and then 5 minutes off to refresh to use the bathroom to get some water and then it goes off and I'm back at it again whatever project I'm working on if I put that block time those 90 minutes in my calendar and I do three sessions of that Pomodoro it helps me overcome my ADHD it focuses is me and because the timer is counting down it's like every 5 minutes I'm like how much did I get done how much did I get done it's almost up it's almost up and it just focus me to get the most work and makes me more creative and more resourceful and doesn't waste my time which brings us to number four which is to commit to others this one very few people use and it's one that I use almost on a daily basis I remember when I was writing my book I tried three times the first time I sat down and said oh I'm going to commit to writing every morning and for 3 weeks I sat there and I wrote and I wrote and I had no process and no purpose and honestly I didn't even know what the book was going to be about then I tried again and I figured you know what maybe I should do this different and I had a friend help me out with the outline but the problem was I was still doing it on my own by the time I gave it my third shot I realized I need somebody else to be committed to so first thing is I hired a person that became my book CEO this person managed me as the talent to be accountable to them second is I hired another writing partner that way I had somebody that was waiting for me on my writing and then I also had somebody that was an editor where we would schedule time together to review the work as a team and on that call we would do the work of writing the book without that I probably wouldn't have created the type of work I did because having somebody else that is a co-creator a collaborator somebody to bring different creative ideas it actually made the whole process fun and I now use that strategy in all the different areas of my life from doing paperwork I don't want to do to creative ideation with some new content ideas with my team to strategic planning instead of doing it solo I always invite one or two other people to collaborate with even when I had no money I would just call a friend and ask them if they wanted to do like joint working sessions sometimes we'd meet up at a coffee shop other times we would just connect over Zoom they have accountability to me because I'm sharing my screen and then we' just set a timer we check in with each other and we just make sure that hey whatever project we said we would move forward we committed to that person cuz it turns out you will do more for other people than you'll ever do for yourself just think about your dog I mean if your dog's sick you're like the world stops and we get that dog healed up and all the medication we give 100% completion of whatever pills the doctor says whereas for you you will go long periods of time sometimes not feeling good without ever taking care yourself let alone if you get medication maybe you'll finish 60 70% of it so understand committing to other people is actually a very powerful tool to keep us focused and be really accountable which brings us to number five which is the schedule time to recharge I'm not proud of this but I used to be the person that would work a 100 hour weeks I would just get up crank ignore everything else ignore my health ignore my friends ignore my relation relationships because I was just doing whatever I could to try to be successful and it did create momentum the challenge is it also created bad momentum because I didn't take the time to recharge to reset and the energy was frantic which means there was no space for what I call step functions of growth ideas so now I have a completely different approach and here's how I think about it just like I have blocked time for work I also have blocked time for rest see the magic that I've discovered is in the reset we are one creative idea away from having our whole life change think about it could be that book idea it could be that idea to call a friend could be a conversation with somebody that you didn't even plan and what I've discovered is the world works this way there's two different systems no matter who you are think about the ying and yang there's a sympathetic system which is fight ORF flight and then there's the parasympathetic system which is to relax and to chill out there's the anabolic system which takes protein to build muscle and then there's a catabolic system that eats the protein there's UST stress and then there's distress UST stress is when you decide to do something hard and you push on the World to Change and distress is when you feel like the world is pushing on you and it makes it hard understanding that if you want to be the most productive the most creative the most expansive that you have to have some push and also some time to pull some drive and also some reset I now have cut off time for when I finish my work because I know my brain's not going to work well after that no good decisions going to be made and I also understand how my body and my energy flows so for example I'll go sit in the hot tub and just sit there on my phone and outline new video ideas or reach out to friends I haven't seen in a while or think about creative ideas of things I might want to do over the next 6 months it's understanding that you can't always be going charge charge charge because it's actually in the reset where you create the space that makes the creativity and the big needle moving decisions come to life I've learned over the years that vacations make me a better person for my team for my family so when I go away and I go mountain biking with my friends or I take my family and we go to somewhere is when I come back I just feel energized I feel that I've got more life to give other people and it turns out that if you're building businesses with other people you have Partners you have customers you have vendors those conversations are going to be a lot more fun when you've reset yourself you've new energy you're recharged to attack your work I think most people feel guilty taking time off they think anytime I'm not working or I'm not showing up for my team that I'm just one second away where something massive is going to implode on my life and it makes it really hard for them to unplug where the opposite is actually true it's kind of like a pressure cooker if you work 100 hours a week and you don't take any time off eventually that pressure cooker is going to overflow and you may think that you're good but your body will start going into adrenal fatigue you'll have anxiety attacks I've seen people break out into shingles they've got these little sore spots that start breaking out on their skin and that's literally their body telling them they have not reset they've not created any self-care in their life you can't Redline your body Non-Stop and it not be upset with you productivity is being able to stay consistent for long periods of time without having to reset because you went too far which brings us to number six which is to wake up early this one is going to be controversial but early risers make more money period full stop if you look at any person that's ever succeeded from Church Hill to you know Steve Jobs they get up earlier than most people now I'm not saying you have to join the 500 a.m.

in the morning for you to attack but sometimes you're working on something you didn't finish it so if you didn't just move it to the next time block maybe to the next day or maybe to the afternoon one end of the week I said why didn't you ask for it to get done tomorrow they said well I don't know I go well that's the difference between 4 days in one day and she's like I didn't want to be that person I said well you could at least asked Parkinson's law states the work will expand to the time you allocate to it and that's true for your calendar it's true for other people momentum is about stacking small wins so if you start doing what you say when you're going to do it is going to build that confidence because you're keeping the commitments you made to yourself in private so build that discipline which brings us to number 12 which is to turn off all notifications on your phone some of you guys are like no way Dan I'm not not doing this and I'm going to tell you there are professional PhD doctorate employees working at all these tech companies building all this software that their only job is to distract you and when I think of the most unproductive thing you could possibly do is allow the notifications the apps to interrupt you when you are doing something that you decided was important to you just because somebody else somewhere in the world sent you a message your focus is worth more than somebody else's notification remember one time I was messaging with one of my new employees and he would always reply always reply always reply and I sat down with them when our 101 and I shared with them I said hey I love that you want to be on top of things and respond to me right away but that also tells me that you're easily interrupted if you're doing it with me you're probably doing with other people which means I'm not getting your best work because I'm not getting your focus work your head's down deep in the zone work so I'm going to need you to batch those activities into one block time so that if you need to reply to me just sit down and look at your all text messages and reply and then get back to your next project most people will never do this because they are addicted to the dopamine hits of those notifications but to do your best work you have to get into Flow State and to do that you need to get lost in your work not distracted by those notifications most people are on their phone all day not working because it's an illusion of productivity responding to people's messages feels like you're doing something but you're not getting anything done you're staying busy but you're not creating you're not producing you're not pushing a project forward you're just moving a bunch of information it's not that it doesn't have to get responded to but stacking that and doing it on your own time instead of allowing an app to bug you is going to be a game changer for productivity if you want to learn the 44 cheat codes I know at 44 that I wish I knew at 24 click the link and I'll see on the other side
  — *danmartell: 12 Hacks to Be 99.9% More Productive That Cost Nothing*

other people showed up and couldn't do basic things Dayton's never asked me for a single thing and because he's always shown up and left the car better than I expected I was so impressed by him going looking to remove parts from the requirements from the product because a part that is in the product that doesn't need to exist is one more thing that needs to be managed and Q&A and could break and could cause other issues so he's always looking at the requirements and he's always pulling so much stuff out of it that it should not work and then he says then I added it back to make the part work you're like I want to make it better and then you do something else how can I make it better you add it all back up and the problem is at some point you create this complexity ball it didn't solve a problem so you just keep adding and adding and adding my Approach in business is every time I add something it starts with negative value sometimes you need to think about before you add anything what can I remove so if you want to keep this simple I always say start with one Clear customer you want to serve one clear product or offer some people call them Godfather offers cuz they're so good they can't say no then one clear way of getting attention marketing then one clear way of selling them and then focus on those four for one year those are the five ones if you put all that into your focus which stands for follow one course until successful don't complicate it you'll get rich which brings us to number four which is to give to get and this is a cool one cuz I learned this back when I was in rehab I struggled with addiction ended up in rehab as a teenager and what I learned through this 11 months process obviously becoming a better version of me and dealing with some of the pain that I experienced is that in therapy they talk about if you want to be sober you got to help other people be sober this whole idea is that you can only keep what you give away when I started in business and I thought about like how can I be valuable in business business I realize that if I want more money I got to help other people make more money if you think about it that all your income is probably about 10% of the value created in the world I call this a 10% rule that whatever value created in the world you're getting 10% of that so if you're making a million dollars you've made other people $10 million so the more you give the more you get see most people have a scarcity mindset so they don't want to give anything away they're just like if I give that then I don't got it but that's get to 2 million you might run around the day like you're spinning plates and you're worried that one of them's going to drop and smash all over the place but you've got leaders that are managing people to scale the 10 million you got to be good at communicating through outcomes not tasks because the outcome is is where the person now has a vision for what they got to do and it's up to them to figure out the task the worst thing you can do is hire somebody and tell them what to do you want to hire somebody and have them tell you what to do and that's how you go from 2 to 10 million and that's the biggest thing is hiring driven people learning to work through them and let them figure out how to solve problems at scale cuz the volume 2 to 10 I mean if you're a barber you might have 10 barber shops not one or two managing 10 that's a completely different set of complexity that you got to figure out because if you don't your ceiling of complexity will be where you naturally hit a glass ceiling of growth might be at 300,000 1.5 million even 10 million and then 30 million there's all different levels of these glass ceilings of growth or what I call the complexity ceilings that you have to learn how to deal with and overcome if every time you hire somebody new you start first with the premise that they have to buy time out of your calendar something you're currently doing then as you grow and you hire new people your calendar should get Freer and Freer and Freer see most people don't do this they hire for capacity I need another logo designer I need another programmer I need another Roofing guy the challenge is if your early days then what happens is you still do the work and then you get stuck with all the other stuff as well and you can burn yourself out where you're literally working 30 40 hours a week billing or doing customer facing stuff fulfillment delivery and then you spend the other 30 40 hours a week trying to run run the business so the best way is to start with your calendar find out what you're doing that is low cost to pay somebody else to do that you don't enjoy doing in the first place and build the process the standard operating procedure to do that work so that you're not present and give it to somebody else and I'll tell you the first day you don't do all your invoicing you don't do all your follow-ups you don't do scheduling and somebody else takes those tasks and gets them done for you and they're done as good if not better because they actually have the time to do it right where you were rushing before it is a beautiful moment My Philosophy is 80% done by somebody else is 100% freaking awesome cuz I'll tell you the faster you get to a point where you're making money that's not connected to your time that will be the freedom point where business becomes fun and you can get there a lot faster than you think if you use that process of looking at your calendar and only hiring people that's going to buy time out of your calendar first most people over complicate the training of the new person and I think it's because they want to be seen as like competent you start off and you're like I don't want somebody to think that I'm incompetent but the truth is if all the person does the first day they start is sit there and watch videos of you doing the work that you've been doing that they now have to take over then you didn't have to train them and then it's up to them to tell you what they learn watching those videos but that might be the first week or two weeks so yes you're thinking oh I can't afford to pay somebody to do that you can't afford not to do it because if it costs you your time when you hire somebody new to get them up to speed where they can actually create value for your business then you will accept yourself to have an underperformer on your team because that underperformer is easier to deal with than not having them and having to find and train somebody new because it takes your time but I think that part is really where people overco complicated it doesn't have to be a fancy document that's thought through all the different scenarios and can be as simple as just a bunch of record of you doing the work and having them watch you do it and then figure out what makes sense for them and then the last step is to exit Here's My Philosophy if you don't build a business to sell then you'll always feel like you're a prisoner of your company but a business that you could sell is a great business to operate so in many ways just having the decision that someday I will sell this changes your whole relationship with the energy of building the business it doesn't mean you're less passionate or not committed to the Future what you're doing is saying Hey I want this thing to be bigger than me I don't want to be needed for this thing to add value to people I love in my community online in the world I want it to continue past my time if something happens to me where I get hit by a bus I don't want the business to not keep going so I think that if you start with the premise that someday somebody's going to buy this it's going to be worth something and realize that for most entrepreneurs the real Payday is by creating a business that is valuable to somebody else then they should want to create it to exit you can sell your business for anything doesn't matter what industry you're in restaurants Home Services tech there is a business out there in your kind of industry similar to yours that is sold for a billion dollars so the amount you could sell it for is literally whatever you want if you're willing to do the work but it starts with the belief that hey somebody could build it if I learn how to build it in a way that's valuable to somebody else the only reason is valuable is if it makes profit consistently without you needing to be there that's what makes it valid people don't buy businesses as charity projects they buy businesses because whatever amount they gave you it generates a return on that amount over a period of time and they expect it to grow when you're at a million they're not going to pay you a whole lot if you're 5 million that's the beginning of where it's interesting to other people if you're at 10 million all of a sudden now people are like oh I'd pay you three four five six times on your profit your eida if you're at 50 million that's like real lifechanging dollar amount that's the cool part it's not like one day maybe I'm going to have this big payday and all a sudden I'm going to live differently is like if you do it right and the business makes profit from the first year second year third year Etc your life changes right away the more the business can one operate without you there 100% if you need to be there every day that's not as valuable two two it doesn't make profit if it makes a lot of profit that's obviously awesome three there's a business operating system a way you do it think about your company way you've documented the process for how you do production management and planning and accounting and recruiting essentially if somebody bought your business they could go through this manual for how you've been able to create the income and the profit and the whole team structure that's really valuable and then you're in an industry that's growing that's also very valuable ideally if you have some intellectual property or some patents or something that's kind of unique to you guys and how you do it that nobody else has that makes it obviously very attractive to a potential buyer but those are the big things you want to think about it's stable durable Revenue talent in the business is incentivized to stick around that you're not needed for it to operate and grow and then a manual that defines the process for how you get customers sell those customers and keep those custom customers if you can do those things then you have a business that's valuable and people pay you top dollar to buy you so there's a bunch of different processes but the easiest one is to just reach out to a business broker depending on your industry there's somebody that has a job that his full-time job is to evaluate your business tell you what you need to fix to make it better and then go find somebody that would strategically or financially buy your business because it makes sense for them to expand their revenue and they will sit down with you for the most part for free to evaluate and assess where you're at and tell you hey go work on these things and then I could probably present you to other businesses and you can do it in a way that's Anonymous so your customers don't find out but there's a whole market for doing this so the interesting about selling a business is most people don't know what to do with the money and I'll tell you why if you are making 5 million a year in your business and a million in profit that's 20% profit on $5 million so if some some gives you $5 million where are you going to take that 5 million and invest it to get a 20% return so the fascinating part for most entrepreneurs selling the business economically doesn't make a lot of sense because then they don't have a vehicle that going to continue to pay them that kind of return usually what people do in these long journeys of building the business is they will diversify into other means of businesses you hear people say like multiple streams of income the truth is is the multiple streams are like real estate Consulting passion projects or businesses they co-found with other people but if you have those then you would take the capital that you just got from selling your business and you would reinvest it in those other businesses because again you own a piece of those business or they are your business and that's going to generate a better return if you don't have that then hopefully you have a wealth manager if you don't you should get one that's a fiduciary CU if not they're just going to sell you stuff cuz they make money off of it not a good look but it's also an opportunity for you to learn the next skill creating wealth is a different and essentially it's a process or different components so that you can communicate the depth of a character and I use this exact strategy to build my personal brand so that people get to know me because what we want to do is we want to break the trust barrier so there's four components the first one is understanding what is your North Star what are you uniquely known for think about people like Elon Musk he wants to colonize Mars Gary ve wants to buy the Jets everybody that you follow online you know what their North Star is but for a lot of content creators they don't ever tell anybody what that is the other side of the character diamond is their Kryptonite right it's the flaw see some people are such Perfection as and experts they don't want anybody to know that they have any dark side or the shadow side I mean personally my thing and I'm a big Fitness person I do Ironman I work out is I love chocolate and peanut butter it is one of my favorite things I'm also a big fan of com kill Tony is one of my favorite shows on the Internet it's what I watch to go to bed every night and I mean think about Elon elon's favorite thing to do is memes he's got some Kryptonite right and then quirky what is something about you that you would share that would make the person feel that's kind of quirky my personal one is that I kind of have a secret addiction to Real Housewives and I mean if you want to get into the debate of which one's my favorite I'd have to probably put New Jersey in the Hat followed by a close Beverly Hills but that's my Quirk what is your Quirk I had a friend his thing is he cooks himself fresh made pasta every night it's just a little something that makes somebody uniquely them and then finally what are your passions what do you love to do for me I permission to share all aspects of me so if you're struggling on being you give yourself permission to be yourself and unlike anybody else using the character Diamond you'll be able to demonstrate who you truly are at your core and have your audience trust you so a lot of people have these belief blockers about content I want to break them apart these are the three biggest fears and how to reframe them for you to take action the first one is your life isn't that interesting here's the deal is you've gone through some challenges some setbacks and your best content will be you sharing those transformation those moments where you overcame the second belief blocker is that I don't have anything of value to share I'm just getting started Ed I don't feel like I'm that smart I believe that every person has done something impressive with their lives and whatever you know is going to help everybody that's just behind you you want to share that you want to literally give it all away if you're a consultant share all your secrets I think information should be given away for free and then people will pay for implementation the third blocker is what are other people going to think your high school friends your co-workers whatever it is here's what I know if your Ambitions to help people and create content is bigger than your fears then your life will be way bigger than your dreams do not hold back from sharing your true unique giftedness with the world CU that's how we create connection the second step is what I call the opens I remember I was talking to a client of mine and they were like you know I don't have enough leads and I need to do more marketing and I opened up their profile and I was like you literally have 6,000 followers I go have you messaged every one of them no why would I do that every one of your followers are essentially an optin for somebody's shown interest to want to learn from you and you haven't even started a conversation with them so here's how you turn those followers into leads this is the opener framework and it is awesome so there's two big strategies first we start with inbound the inbound is a handraiser on your social media account ask people if they want something of value that you've created it could be a template a blueprint a script any kind of Playbook and saying if you want this then just message me and I'll send it to you that's the inbound cuz they're messaging you interact with those people and see if you can be helpful the second is outbound which is all about messaging every new follower first off you say hello to them thanking them for the engagement hey Jane thanks for the follow or thanks for the engagement and then asking them are you here for the content are you looking to and get the results so whatever it is for me it's are you here for the vids are you looking to grow your business are you here for the content are you looking for a new logo whatever it is it's a this or that but always end with the thing you want them to do I keep see see if people mix those up you don't go are you here to grow your business or you're just here for the videos whatever is the thing that you can help people make that the second call to action and when they say both or the second or just the first one you can then engage with them the third step is to qualify see when you go to a doctor and you sit down with them they don't just say like what do you want and then they give you a prescription and then you walk out the reason why is any prescription without diagnosis is malpractice so for you to try to sell something to somebody and you don't even know if it's really the right thing for them or if you can actually help them it's not only wrong it'll waste your time so I want to teach you a framework that's going to help you get through it so that you can move to the next step only if the person is ready for you so there's four steps to qualifying somebody the first step is asking them about their desire so you might say you know where do you want to be by the end of the year or what is your dream weight or what is a ideal outcome for your new brand and they start telling you about it then you want to go to their current state their reality and ask them like well what does it look like today see if you ask their desire first then you have the permission to ask about their today if you say what is your goal for your revenues and you can say well what do revenues look like today or what do your profit look like today you really want to get descriptive so tell me about your situation how many people do you work with what have you tried so far that kind of stuff then we want to talk about impact so asking them questions like how does this impact your life today how does it impact your time how does it impact your emotions how does it impact other people in your family how does it impact people at your work but really understanding the pain around their current state the reality helps the prospect understand their current situation then once you do that you then earn the permission to ask him this beautiful question do you want my help and that is where we give the permission back to the person to lean in or lean out they either say yes I'd love to learn more or they say I don't really know what you do or I don't even know who you are which gives you an opportunity to try to overcome that objection but you want to get it out of the way up front so you qualify them have them lean in and then you get to to move to the next step that's when we use the buying Zone some people are either too far on the left where they have no hope that they can solve the problem no confidence in themselves or they think that what you're selling is maybe too advanced for them and then you have people on the other side of the spectrum that have too much hope that feel that hey I can figure this out myself or too much confidence like I've done this I can do this myself or or maybe think it's too simple or too Fisher Price for them and what you want to do is you want to use reframes I'm going to teach you in a second to get them into the buying Zone and the buying zone is this perfect place where they have enough trust they have enough hope they feel like it's a perfect fit for them so what do you do when you're on either side of that Spectrum these are the three reframes that I use to get the prospect in the buying Zone first off a comparison reame so some people be like I don't know if I could ever be a millionaire and you might ask them you say do you know what percentage of the entrepreneurs are millionaires or how many millionaires are in the US they go no they go there's actually 22 million millionaires in the US do you think it's possible that maybe you could be one of them wow I didn't know there were so many that's a comparison reframe or a calculation reframe so some people they might have a lot of confidence in what they're doing and I might ask them a question like well what are your profits at and they'll go like oh we haven't made any profit so that's somebody had too much hope and you want to bring them back a little bit to get them into the buying zone or you might ask them to like talk about Revenue numbers so it's like what's your goal how many units would you have to sell to hit your goal when people people start running the numbers in their head they go from not enough confidence to confidence because we say hey that's only like five more units per month so that's a calculation reframe then another one that I love using is a correlation reframe and that's like for example with business coaching or people that hire agencies or other experts to help them you might say like wouldn't it be weird if you met an athlete at the Olympics that said they didn't have a coach they go yeah that'd be pretty weird well do you want to be somebody that performs at the highest level or at the lowest level and that helps somebody going from too much confidence to just perfect to allow them to make the decision to purchase the fourth step is make the offer most people get stalled at this level because they don't feel like what they can do to help somebody is valuable enough or it's need to be packaged and all this whizbang marketing site and copy and it's got to have a video and all these things I will tell you every one of my software companies all my coaching companies everything I've ever sold I sold it before I ever designed it or built it over time you can develop it over time you can add all the bells and whistles but you just want to go out there and have people lean in make an offer validate that what you have is valuable to them by them transacting and then from there keep going when I launched this new coaching program I didn't even have the call scheduled I didn't have a payment link on stripe all I had was an outline of something that I was thinking of doing I copied and paste it I sent it people said I'm in then I had to go call my team create a link send it to them and then we got it going these are the two things that are needed to create a great offer first off is you have to map to pain whatever the conversation you've had with them and all the pain points that they're saying is a struggle from my team doesn't listen to I don't have Clear Vision or I feel unmotivated or I don't feel consistent whatever the pain is your offer needs to map to the pain and overcome those concerns overcomes those objections so that they feel like this is a killer offer so once you have an offer outline I'll give you a template below that you can just copy and paste and play with keep it simple I want to make this simple cuz I think complexity fails simple scales that's number one number two is you either win the person decides to buy from you or you follow up because fortunes are made in the followup probably 50% of the revenue that I have is because I kept them on a list on my phone and I kept following up I kept showing up I kept adding value I kept doing hand raisers I kept sharing wins I kept doing the things that prove to the people following me that I was able to deliver on the thing that we had chatted about and eventually say I'm ready let's do this these are the three things that actually hold people back from selling the first one is they're too transactional there's no empathy it's almost like back and forth without conversation the key there is to Echo the feelings whatever the person says to you you want to acknowledge that send it back so what I hear is this did I get it right yes perfect and move on the second one is you don't have the proper expert frame and what you get is get put in the friend zone so when you're chatting with somebody you might ask them what their goals are and they go what are your goals that is the friend zone you don't want to be put there instead you want to lead the conversation you want to take control of it it is actually perfectly okay to not even acknowledge a question and just keep moving the conversation to the next level of qualifying so you can get the person to a place where you can maybe be of help the third is just being lazy I see people all the time they're like in momentum and they're chatting and they're making revenue and then they get busy with the business and then they get non-responsive and they drop the ball and they don't follow up and that is probably the biggest killer of Revenue and that's why in The Next Step I'm going to teach you the process you got to follow to make this easy the fifth step is process see the secret that no one else talks about is that sequencing equals success so when we're doing chat when we're having sales conversations you just can't just go all over the place and talk to the hottest qualified lead and try to move that forward and Skip all around you need a process and for me I do the reverse of what most people do see most people do their opens then they qualify and then they make offers my Nuance here is to go from offers so what I do is I start with everybody I've made an offer for in the past maybe yesterday the day before and I process From The Bottom the old this all the way up to the top then I go to qualified so these are people that have already qualified and I start at the bottom I go the way up top and then I go and I do my opens and I do my leads that way I'm always moving the most qualified pipeline forward to increase Reven Revenue if I only have 45 minutes on chat that's what I'm doing now Instagram actually makes this really easy if you go into your business account they have these things called Tags or labels and the way I do it is that if I find somebody leaning in I tag them as a lead then if I qualify them I tag them as booked then if I make the offer I tag them as ordered and If eventually buy I change it to paid that's way they're green and I put them in my primary inbox in my Instagram because now they're a client so if they chat with me I know to prioritize those messages that's how I made 3 million on Instagram DMS if you want to learn the only four skills you need to build a billion dollar company click the link and I'll see you on the other side

- this is how you build product that people pay you for trip over themselves to pay you for it without breaking the bank see I've been building software companies now for 20 years I buy software companies I coach some of the world's top CEOs and I've learned over the years spending tens of millions of dollars on engineering teams product managers you know all the tooling the fancy kanban boards project management you know simulation software all of that it really comes down these five core principles that if you implement into your product process will help you build Innovation from insights you learn from your customers that will continue to keep you competitive you don't listen to this I know you're gonna end up failing because you won't have product marketing I'm not even being dramatic you literally maybe have some traction today and then you're gonna hit a ceiling where there's more people flying out the door than you're able to bring in and you're going to get frustrated and you're going to give up you're going to decide to either shut it down or sell it for pennies This Is How We Do number one is Define your product Vision see a lot of Founders maybe this is you or maybe your product manager and you work with a CEO founder is they have the vision but it's up in their head okay my rule when I coach CEOs is you need to communicate where you want to be in 18 months clearly we're talking like high level road map so that the rest of the team can make decisions that's aligned with where you want to go your vision see if you've got a team even if it's six people like every day they wake up to make the product better to fix bugs to talk to customers and if you don't give them any direction then essentially you're hoping that they see what you've never put out visually and execute against that it's why a lot of CEOs get frustrated when their team make decision you're like why did you do that you didn't Define your product Vision you didn't sit down and actually describe this is the problem that we're going to solve this is the customer segment we're solving it for here's our unique perspective on how how we're going to solve it and we're going to get everybody aligned with this meaning every person in the company is going to be pulling on the rope in the same direction because if everybody's pulling on the rope but they're pulling in different directions guess what you make no movement and very few companies have a detailed definition of their product Vision that they can use continuously every week in their product meetings to let everybody know hey remember guys we're going over here and this is the problem we're solving and this is our unique perspective on the problem and these are the customers we want to serve and anytime the sales team brings in people that don't look like those customers can we can we ask them to stop and anytime the marketing team puts together a marketing program that attracts people just because they get cheap leads but it doesn't get our best fit customers are ready to pay customers we ask them to stop all of this will allow you to just get better leaner and faster if you have a clearly defined product Vision number two is create a road map see some of you guys have maybe never built software before it's your first time maybe you've tried it in the past and you know a lot of entrepreneurs have this like Steve Jobs complex they're like I know what the customer wants and they're wrong and if they if I listen to them I would just build a faster horse all this craziness okay a product roadmap is very simple it's over the next short term typically six months maybe nine months you can tell your team this is our priority most importantly it tells your customers what you're going to build and what sequence right my favorite thing to do is actually ask customers for feedback on kind of product feature enhancement and then map that feedback to the thing I'm gonna go build that when I launch it because it's part of the roadmap and everybody knows I then tell the customers it was their idea okay like sneaky deeky trick that could transform the way you create raving fans you can't do that if you don't have a product roadmap okay it's different than a vision a road map is shorter and it just lets everybody know this is what we're focusing on right now this is what we're going to focus on next all your crazy ideas dump them into this category of stuff and then every time we sit down to talk about what we're going to work on next we can pull from there to figure it out prioritize it have a different process for it but a road map is a critical tool that the best software companies out there know how to manage and communicate to the rest of their team number three create cross-functional teams see most companies that struggle with really great product it's because they have an engineer that's maybe in some other part of the world and they're writing code and every once in a while they throw stuff over the fets and then you're like okay now we gotta test it and we play with it and we find all the bugs and then we like send it back over the fence and hopefully they fix My Philosophy is to create pod structures and a pod structure is very cool because it allows a group of people to have everybody they need to actually ship working code see if you have like oh well this is a person that does the the customer stories and then that goes to a designer and then that goes to an engineer and then the engineer needs to talk to their CTO and figure out like how does this get built into our infrastructure that's where the breakage happens right whereas most of the best software companies in the world the Facebooks the Googles Amazon they use a pod structure scrum talks about this Agile development talks about this where they can talk to the customer directly take that feedback put together a product spec build it in-house be responsible for it my favorite example is this is a HubSpot where they would do I don't know what they called it but essentially show and tell so every two weeks they would invite all the different pods to show their work now here is the criteria you couldn't show unless it was actually in staging couldn't have been clickable prototypes it can't be ideas if you want to demo on these show and tell days you have to have working product in staging ready to go that everybody's like that's amazing ship it to production right and just allowing this process of continuously upgrading and refining so there's no bottlenecks I mean most people don't know this but companies like Shopify Facebook and many others ship to Productions hundreds of times per day they've built their infrastructure where if somebody has an idea and they have the time in that day they can literally write the code change the thing submit it it gets approved shipped to production there's no no release schedule there's no like oh this is our quarterly release if there's a bug they fix it it's gone and the only way to do that is if you have cross-functional teams number four is you've got to monitor progress and metrics okay so at the end of the day it's not about lines of code that matter it's about did the customer receive more value did the code base get more stable did our company achieve their kpis or metrics we set out for you know at the end of the day business very simple how many customers come in how much do they pay us how many people stick around very simple I know some of you guys will be like oh yeah well I measure this metric and this metric and this metric it's like super Advanced and nerdy stuff which I love but I also know that at the simplest form if we can just instrument those different data points and say how many customers did we get in the last month did they activate did they pay us did they stick around and then as you deploy new features see your your product features should only be created if they solve a problem if they improve one of those metrics you know a lot of people have good ideas but when you say well how is this going to help the business and they're like well it'll make our customers happy okay is it going to keep more customers that are canceling well no but it's going to make the ones that are staying more happy okay well we've got you know eight nine ten percent of the people every month leaving could we start with fixing those problems could we pull the report what I call the customer cancellation survey and actually reverse engineer what are top customers that we would have liked to have kept why are they leaving and fixing those holes first too often people confuse activity with progress to me the way we avoid this is we instrument our code base everybody monitors those kpis that are important to us including Speed and Performance there's nothing worse than releasing new software I remember one time our team released this big search engine change and it's like all these advanced search features and they were so pumped and then when I tested it out it was three times slower it doesn't matter how cool your search is if it slows down the whole thing customers don't care about that Jeff Bezos says it best he goes look all I know in the future is our customers will always want cheaper prices delivered faster think about that cheaper prices delivered faster so if your thing makes the site faster if it makes it that we have more product inventory if it makes the price cheaper if it makes it faster to get to somebody those are all really good things you should focus on so monitor progress and the kpis and metrics and that's how we create the Baseline for everybody else to make the product better number five is iterate and improve see when you get into larger engineering teams you will discover that many of them have this thing called devops and devops is a department that allows the engineers to request ways that the the devops team can make the writing of the software easier better just faster for them to develop new features and one of my favorite examples is this uh feature that Facebook has called gatekeeper and what gatekeeper does it allows you to deploy new features but to focus on different demographics so if I wanted to test some new let's say I want to change the color of the the like variations that you weren't originally intending to offer or really feel like they need then that is an area where you can hone in and create clear deliverables the reason that this leads to owners are paralyzed by the fear of making the wrong moves but here's what I've seen and I've seen it time and time again success isn't about avoiding mistakes it's about making them faster and learning from them quickly the owner who tests their offers fast get their marketing message out into the world and then adjust just based on real feedback they're the ones who actually win and they win a whole lot faster than those who don't say anything at all to their Market this leads perfectly into truth number six which I'm going to say might be a hard pill to swallow now before I share the next truth let me ask you something how many great ideas do you have sitting in your notes app right now I know I have probably a ton truth six is about to make you feel uncomfortable about that truth number six is that people judge you by your actions not by your intentions the market doesn't care about your potential it doesn't care about your perfect plan it only cares about what you actually put out into the world I see so many owners that are kind of stuck in like planning mode or that someday thinking but your clients your team your family they all respond to the things that you do not the things that you plan to do and the truth becomes even more critical when you understand truth number seven now this next truth it's the one that usually makes people the most uncomfortable because it forces you to look at something that you might actually be avoiding truth number seven your time is worth more than money when I started I said yes to every project because I was afraid to leave money on the table I don't know if that sounds familiar or not but here's the math that changed everything for my clients that $33,000 project that takes 40 hours it's actually costing you $115,000 in opportunity cost you can always make more money but you can't make more time that's something I learned from Alan Weiss who wrote the Consulting Bible this realization leads directly into truth number eight which shows you how to actually protect that time if you're starting to realize how valuable your time is you're going to love this next truth because it shows you exactly how to make every hour count truth number eight is the quality of your life comes down to the quality of your daily habits everyone's looking for a big breakthrough moment but after two decades in this game I can tell you that it's not about major moves it's about what you do every single day the morning routine that keeps you sharp the weekly planning that prevents the fires the daily content that builds your Authority these small habits compound over time and allow you to create massive results my friend Phil Graham says you have to behave your way to your goals but again these habits only stick if you understand truth number nine now before I share the next truth think about the last time you felt burnt out because truth nine explains exactly why that probably happened truth number nine your business can't outrun your personal Foundation most owners think that their next breakthrough is hiding in some fancy strategy or new tactic I love tactics I love strategies I love tools but I've watched hundreds of owners hit the same wall that's why I take Fridays off I mountain bike on Wednesdays I have coffee with my wife most mornings and these aren't actually luxury items they're the foundation that lets me show up as the CEO that my business actually needs the last truth it's the one that kind of ties all of this stuff together and it might be the most counterintuitive one of them all truth number 10 is lighten up yep lighten up this is supposed to be fun and it's never as bad as it seems when things are rough or as amazing as it feels when things are flying high and everything seems to be going great the most successful owners have this weird superpower if you will and they care really deeply about the outcomes while staying oddly detached from any single specific result they treat their business more like an adventure and not a burden your next client isn't your last chance that project hiccup isn't the end of the world the moment you lighten up is the moment that things actually start to flow for you now that you know these 10 truths which one resonated with you the most let me know in the comments below and if you're ready to see how I help agency owners Founders service providers Implement these principles to build build businesses they actually enjoy then check out the link in the description or go ahead and watch this video
  — *greghickman: 10 Business Truths I Wish I Knew Before Making $1M*

- in this video I want to break down how we've been able to stand out in a crowded Marketplace to attract more of our ideal clients while still charging a premium and actually keeping clients longer because of it if you don't know who I am my name is Greg Hickman I'm the founder of alt agency and we've helped over 700 agencies consultants and service providers to productize their service and monetize their expertise one of the ways we do that is with the tool that I'm going to introduce to you that will help you position your services around the things that your clients your deal clients value the most about 18 months ago we started to experience a little bit of a plateau in our growth after a handful of years that were pretty steady in fact we started to get to a point where we were losing some clients and so that sent us on a little bit of a hunt we started researching and interviewing clients that had churned out that maybe weren't happy all the way to clients that have loved us and have been with us for many many years we started looking at all of the data and the information and what we were able to conclude was that our message to the market was actually misaligned with the value that our best clients were sharing back to us meaning the Angles and the hooks and the promises of the marketing were I'll say sexy and even say things that maybe other people were saying where we kind of started to blend in with others and when they finally came in the clients that stayed with us forever got what they actually needed which was slightly different from what they you know attracted them to us in the beginning and then obviously the clients that had this one message of like scale scale scale and came in and were able to get a bunch of time back and be happier on their personal life because of systems and Automation and more profit they actually were not as happy because they were like scale growth mode and the happiest clients were like sweet I've got my life back because I've used these systems and I'm happier in my business Etc and so the message on the front didn't align with the actual value that our happiest clients were reiterating back to us once they started working with us so that led us to zoom out and really start to think about if we want to get more of the clients that we love and that love us how do we make sure that we bring the message the things that they value most to the front of our marketing I say all this because in today's world I mean if you're watching this video you've seen the Tik toks the Instagrams the the YouTube videos on how to get 50 to 100 appointments a day or a week or like some of these ridiculously large and outrageous claims and if I'm being honest with you I hit a point where I kind of felt like we had to play that game to make acquisition work and the reality is is while it did work to get leads it got a lot of the wrong leads and more specifically a lot of the wrong clients and so when we did that deep work of talking to our best clients and the ones that turned out to understand like where were we misaligned it became very very clear all of this became accelerated with this tool they basically said and this this article they created this I guess around March 19th 2020 it was probably before that but they approach this from the perspective of the pandemic but I think it still applies and so they say this is the time for B2B companies to focus on six elements of value and you can see here there's a lot of elements of value and they've highlighted just six now there are obviously a handful of elements as you go from the bottom of the pyramid it goes from objective to subjective and so you want to have a mix your messaging your offer your deliverables should check off a variety of these elements of value from both objective and subjective so you can see down here at the bottom of the pyramid I'm going to take you to my iPad here in a minute but there's table Stakes which clearly like basically everyone needs to just address the table Stakes then there's functional value which I think as you can see here they didn't highlight any of anything from functional value but these are probably things that you might want to focus on for your business then they have the category of ease of doing business and then individual value and inspirational value the company here they say I think this is a fictitious company they're going to focus on messaging and deliverables around risk reduction flexibility stability availability reduced anxiety and then social responsibility so yes could they go to market market and talk about you know things like Time Savings yes but if they interviewed their clients like we did and they weren't emphasizing hey the biggest value that you've given us is giving us Time Savings and their marketing is All About Time Savings you can see how that's probably misalignment with attracting more of the ideal clients so if all of your clients are like hey thank you so much one of the most valuable things to us is you know the fact that you've now reduced our risk or you've made our business more stable or you've reduced our anxiety those are the things that you want to obviously put back into your messaging but not only that if that's where your clients are saying they're getting the most value it also is going to be an indicator on maybe how else can you double down on your deliverables on your services on your product to further help them with the things that they value most so if it's not obvious already I think it's very important for you to interview your most successful clients and most definitely the clients that were not happy that maybe you weren't able to deliver for and in the description below I'm you click over this basically the definition is help the customer increase Revenue which if you scroll around Facebook pretty much everybody talks about this one so it's very functional so you might do that but if all of your clients are telling you yes youd helped us make more money but the real value is insert one of these other things maybe you don't need to to go to market with only making your message around increasing Topline you might have your client come in and say oh my God you save us so much time and you might not have talked about Time Savings in your marketing or the tools or the part of your service that actually helps give your clients time back so how do you save time for users for their overall experience and again as you can see in this pyramid that's table Stakes right functional value this is extremely objective ease of doing business these are pretty objective and you start Crossing into very subject Ive like reducing anxiety growth and development like if you're a company that is about really skill transfer or like a training company you might actually want to lean into growth and development if you are also at the highest level the very subjective inspirational value you could be offering up hope you give your clients and Market hope and then there's obviously the social responsibility which many of you guys have seen in a variety of brands in value has Vision hope social responsibility but if I scroll down there's also an element of value for B to C and I do think that there are some interesting things and interesting ways to look at your business even through the lens of B Toc if you are a B2B provider for example inside of this lifechanging elements section I know that in our business as working with agencies one thing thing that we hear a lot is that agency owners are lonely like entrepreneurship is lonely and being around other Founders that are on a similar path is really really beneficial and that is really like the for us we heard a lot from our clients that like being around others was really really important and it has been a benefit to their life because you know we have training and coaching programs with a already have that you love that have been with you for a while and maybe have the highest LTV you want to understand what they value the most and so coming out of our interviews we were able to see necessarily focus on so some of the things that we lean into a little bit is the fun and the entertainment because we want to build our business around life and you might say oh well a lot of other so that they can live a really fulfilling life and when you get the agency owner who's like yes I want to grow and make more money so that I can spend more time with my kids and have hobbies that I love your marketing message will look different than if the person is like a scale or die type of person who's like I want to have you know a 10 billion a year company and I'm willing to go all in I'm and they're not making enough to make all of this work even worth it like they would have made more still at their job so for most of our clients they want to on the low side have an income of 150 to 200k a year you know after taxes but here's what's fascinating when you build around your ideal schedule and then your energy you usually end up making way more than the minimum right so we want to look at the floor that doesn't mean we can't make more but we got to know that we're in it for a certain amount of payoff here's the truth if you compromise your schedule and your energy just for money you will end up like every other stressed out unfulfilled business owner wondering why success feels so empty so let me show you how to turn these numbers you know these currencies into the real business so now we're going to look at the next phase which is product ation now here's where most service providers get productization completely wrong they just bundle their deliverables and they call it a package and they think that that means they're selling a product so I want to show you the difference between a real world example so let's take I'll take one of the clients that we've worked with it's a marketing agency here's how they used to present their service so before it was the monthly marketing package right it included things like social media management uh email marketing content creation monthly reporting and they got two strategy called per month maybe that sounds familiar but here's the problem clients don't actually want any of those things they want what those things will do for them right so they don't want social media management email marketing and all of that they want what all of these things will give them what the outcome will be if they actually do those things so when we work with our clients here's an example of what the after might look like for that client this might be the predictable pipeline system see now it's a product right it's not a package and the outcome of that package is to fill your pipeline every month without spending hours on content creation or social media okay now look you might actually still do social media management content creation you're doing it for them which ends up filling their pipeline which is what they really want right so they don't care how you fill their pipeline as long as you fill their pipeline now the whole angle of you know without spending hours on content creation that's just leaning into what is this thing that they you know the reason why they're not doing it why are they not filling their pipeline oh content creation is hard I don't want to be on social media or I don't know how to be on social media and so they they end up not doing it so the problem is we want to move away from just the things that we do and transform it into the outcome that they're going to get same deliverables completely different positioning instead of selling the how they're going to do it we're going to sell the what the actual outcome is that the clients want and that they're going to get and here's what happens when you do something like that our clients who have done this kind of shift from package to productization they've been able to raise their price from say 3K to 10K per month clients stopped haggling over deliverables because you're not selling deliverables you're selling an outcome sales conversations actually got shorter because you're getting straight to the point of what they want and results actually improve because clients were more aligned and better aligned with the thing that they were really coming to you for the outcome and so that becomes your core service right in the productization phase I believe the goal is to take that offer in this case the predictable pipeline system and get that to producing somewhere between 25 and 30k a month for your business before moving on to the next phase so you want to kind of get some stability you want to you know get that offer to say 25 to 30k a month where you see like for at least three months in a row you're doing that and you're going to feel confident to move into the next phase which the next phase is IP building now this is where a lot of the magic starts to happen and this is where I think things get really interesting so once you have your core solution dialed in again it's doing 25 to 30k per month mon you're going to take that exact same system and you're going to transform it into more of a done with you offer and so let's talk about how that works so let's say your done for you solution the predictable pipeline system that we just talked about say you sell that for 15K and you're helping clients get their pipeline filled with their ideal prospects each and every month and you're the one doing all of that implementation work that's great but there is a whole Market rather of people who they can't afford that solution so if you're charging 50k they probably can't afford 15K there's also people in your Market that look like your ideal Prospect but they already have teams in house that can maybe handle some of that the done for you handle some of the implementation meaning they need more of the strategy they need the system the road map than more than they need the actual labor hours of you doing it so here's how that math could play out on your path to a million so your starting point again is the core solution is now getting you to 30k a month right so 30k a month at 12 months that's 360 annual from that predictable pipeline system that we were talking about which is really just two core solution sales per month right so you have an offer that's 15K you sell two of those a month that's the 30k a month and you can do two a month every single month for 12 months that's 360k annually earned which means we have a $640,000 gap this is where with the IP we create a training program you can call it a high ticket program you can call it a Consulting program you can call it training you can call it coaching you can call whatever want but it fundamentally it is you walking them through your system showing them how giving them the more that would be a suitable entry point for people that were less qualified than the people that we originally had so people that couldn't necessarily afford 15 to 25k but they could afford 7 500 to maybe 10K and we slimmed down exactly what was included and our process to deliver it now we really only were able to do this once we had delivered our core capability dozens upon dozens upon dozens of times so we had a lot of reps we saw what worked we knew what someone at that level would actually need and they didn't need some of the fancy stuff that we were doing for these higher level people that had a little bit larger of a business so this new market that we came down to we had a more affordable price but it also brought us into the world of people that weren't necessarily at the same scale as our Flagship offer our Flagship service and so this led to a lot of new clients that came in the door and it was really a win-win we were able to serve a new clientele that opened up the amount of people we could actually serve but it also took us way less effort and way less people to actually deliver on the result because we were able to slim it down and standardize it so what did that fundamentally do for us it kind of added a whole new Revenue stream repurposing and re-imagining how we currently did the work that we had been doing for a while so we had our top tier clients doing 15 to 25k so average of 20K and then we had a whole nother level of Provider that we were able to help that was paying us anywhere from 7 500 to 10K now two things here that I want to call out in the beginning stages we did say no to these people and that was really important when we were getting traction we didn't get bogged down with trying to be everything to everybody we had plenty of interest from this audience the people that maybe weren't already doing a million dollars a year in revenue or even maybe half a million but they needed an evergreen webinar funnel or even a live webinar funnel so we waited until we had enough experience and dialed in our own process that allowed us to be confident in moving down Market I see a lot of people do this way too soon they just want to serve that client that maybe can't afford them and so they basically sell the exact same service that they normally would charge for 20K for and they do it just for half the price because that's what the person's budget was and I actually don't recommend that the slimming down the standardization the client who is down Market likely doesn't need all of the bells and whistles and customizations that you're probably currently doing for your top tier clients and that was very true for us and we see that to be true for a lot of our clients so once we had done again probably 50 different clients we were able to start seeing what a slim down version could look like for someone that was a few steps behind the current Avatar now one other area that was really interesting is because we had built these things so many times we had a lot of Sops and we had a lot of templates that were pre-built meaning that the actual fulfillment on our side was about 75 to 80 percent complete before we ever even accepted the client which made our fulfillment process even faster so you likely have the ability to as you go Downstream also deliver it in a way more leveraged way because you're going to be utilizing hopefully pre-loaded assets assets that you've already created that have worked for your main clients down to this lower Market in some cases this is a great opportunity to add on a done with you version meaning hey we're going to import these campaigns into your active campaign app and then we're going to guide you through customizing the emails and getting it to be complete and functional and we'll do quality control and all that so this was also the one step that positioned us to now add a done with you delivery model for the same exact outcome that we were doing for 25k but for a much lower price Point opening in the market while also still maintaining really really healthy profit margins so going down Market by standardizing your core service into something that will be more approachable for the you know one or two layers down from the current level of client you serve now is a really great way to start expanding the amount of leads you can take on the amount of clients that you can serve again only after you've done enough reps where you're really confident that you can still do it profitably and you're not going to just give them the the 20K deal for 5K just because that's their budget now the second way is to actually narrow your Market be more specific with who you serve I know a lot of agencies and service providers that are very hesitant to narrow their focus but for us we were in this situation where the more and more we got narrow the easier our clients were able to refer us and if you've read Alex formosi's book 100 million dollar offers and you hear about their avatar for gym launch it was really specific I have to go back and reference the book but it was something very similar to the owner operator gym that had at least one facility with at least one employee that had at least 50 paying members that was wanting to sell weight loss meaning that if they didn't want to sell weight loss they were a questionable fit now I would challenge you to think about your ideal fit right now you probably aren't that specific and in our agency when we were building webinar funnels and things like that we weren't either one thing that we noticed was the clients that turned into the difficult clients the ones that were always demanding things that sort of started to treat us like employees they were the ones where we were dealing directly with the owner and didn't have any sort of help in-house specifically around marketing specifically around the person that would know how to generate a isn't in their best interest because they need specialization on solving specific problems that are catered to you know exactly what their business looks like and what their goals in bottlenecks are
