to in a large part come down to not tactics but to your own identity as understanding that what it means to be an agency today is not the same as it was even three years ago five years ago 25 years - want to share three things that you need to do before you go start hiring someone to take over your sales responsibility [Music] if you're new here welcome my name is Greg i'm the founder of alt agency and I want to share a little bit about my experience when I was trying to get out of sales now if you're the type of person who's you know you're pretty good at sales obviously you're the owner of the business so you know your business best but like it's not your sweet spot it's not where you want to be spending most of your time well that was me and I wanted to get out of sales and so we paid all the different you know High ticket sales Consulting organizations and inquired about when does it make sense and we got a lot of really helpful feedback but none of which was that helpful once we actually went through the process ourselves and we learned a whole lot more and I created what I call the three checks and these are the three checks that you need to check before you are actually ready to hire a salesperson now in our business we are a high ticket Consulting and training organization and I was taking all the sales calls up until six figures a month in Revenue I think there are times and places for the owner to start removing themselves from sales before say a seven figure run rate but that's where a lot of this information I'm about to share with you came from and so the first check that I want you to look at is your margins the financial model check and really the question we need to ask ourselves here is do our profit margins support a sales hire I think one thing that I overlooked and that I think a lot of service providers and agencies Overlook is the cost that is going to incur to bring someone on and it's easy to say well yes this role is going to you know if they close a deal even if I give them commission they're going to pay for themselves but the real issue lies in the rest of your financial model if your cost of goods to deliver for a client are too high if your existing margins are non-existent or Worse maybe you're already losing money and then you bring this person on it's only going to make things worse so if you're operating at say a 10 or 15 profit margin bringing someone on too early is likely going to eat into roughly seven to ten percent of your margin so if you're already not paying yourself as the owner and you don't have a healthy enough profit margin margin it might not actually make sense for you to go bring this person on you might want to go raise your rates possibly lower your operating expenses or your cost to deliver for each additional new client and make sure that you have a more profitable healthy business before you bring this person on the second check is the call volume and availability check this is where we want to look at how many of your current sales slots on your calendar are you actually filling each and every week so when I was going to battle test this for myself I was taking roughly 10 calls a week on top of my other duties and that was leaving me with a full work week so when I obviously was filling those 10 every single week consistently it appeared to be true that I had the amount of volume and the bandwidth to bring someone on now for me those 10 calls were booked and the following week was already getting booked and so it would be a Tuesday the rest of my week would be booked and we'd be booking into the following week so what I did and what I recommend you do is Battle test for a few weeks and at least 30 days to determine do you have the consistent call volume and availability to support bringing on someone else and so what that looked like for me in version one was I committed to validating do we need a salesperson by going all in for at a minimum of 30 days where I was going to take 18 or 20 calls a week which fundamentally each call is an hour plus admin so roughly half of my week I was spending on sales call which by the way is extremely exhausting I did not like it but the business required it and it was the smart thing to do and you might have to do the same so what did I find I found that with the way we were running ads and our organic marketing strategy that I was consistently filling those 20 slots myself each and every week and a little bit into the following week and that was sustainable for a little over 30 days and so I realized that by increasing my ad spend and opening up my calendar availability that I had the ability to put 20 calls per week on the calendar and that's when I realized okay I probably have enough volume to bring someone on now in addition to having the volume what you need to pay attention to is the fact that when you bring this person on they're going to need to get up to speed so you might give them a couple calls a day for the first week or two expect this to be at least a 90-day process of like ramping them up on your offer even if you bring on someone experienced they're gonna come in they're gonna take a couple calls a day so if you had the 20 you're going to still take a handful of those you're going to give some to the new person and you're going to slowly start giving them more as they are closing so fundamentally even though you brought this person on in the first 90 days you should still be taking sales calls as the owner because you kind of need to hedge your bet you need to manage the risk and say look well if I'm taking at least 10 calls and I'm closing 40 percent I'm at least going to bring on four new clients during this onboarding phase and we can get by with those four new clients while I'm getting this person up to speed so you have to measure that because you're not just going to give all of the call volume to this person in the first week or the first month when you need to still hit your numbers remember you're going to eat into your margin and bring this person on and like likely if you're filling your calendar with ads and or marketing you're gonna have to increase your marketing and or your ad spend to fill those that new call availability that you just opened up you've realized one the financial model supports it two you can consistently fill the calendar enough to create an opportunity for a sales rep to be excited about joining your business then the third check is the on track earnings check will this sales person be able to make a six-figure income once up to speed I'd say four to six months with your offer by selling for you can this person make a six-figure salary because most sales reps at least in the agency Consulting space want an opportunity where they're going to be able to have a six figure income and so you have to go back to look at your numbers do you have the volume can they close enough deals at the existing price point with the existing commission to get them to a six-figure on track earnings if they hit the kpis that they need really what we're checking for here is is this job opportunity sexy enough that people are going to want to work for you because because the worst thing that could happen here is you bring someone on you have the call availability and they can't actually reach the outcome that they want to reach from a job and or from an earning potential and so they leave and you have to start this whole process over again which I've already alluded takes time and will cost money and eat into your margin while you're getting this person up to speed so this is a commitment to bring on someone and you really want to run through these three checks before you hire someone I ran through it it was extremely beneficial it is not fun because I need to spend a lot more time on sales calls but I learned a lot and it made training the new salesperson infinitely easier when they did come on so if you're thinking about hiring or a salesperson I highly recommend you follow these three checks it worked for us it works for a lot of our clients those three checks are one the financial model check to make sure you have the margin to support it two the call calendar and availability call volume and availability check to make sure that you can consistently fill a calendar for someone to hit their goals and then with the commission structure the third check is the Entre track earnings check can you offer up a salesperson a desirable job a desirable opportunity to come work for you to hit their financial goals as well inside of your business if you can have those three things and you pass those three checks I think it makes sense to start looking at bringing on a salesperson to really remove yourself from sales so you can focus on the most important thing continuing to grow your business and I can tell you one of the best hires ever is a salesperson because there's no better feeling than being on vacation or out at a meeting and seeing sales coming in because someone else brought them into the business someone else closed them it's the best feeling in the world you really need this hire to grow and scale over time and obviously get your time back so I hope you found this video useful which of the checks do you need to run first are you considering hiring a salesperson let me know in the comments and if you're still interested in learning about how to structure your agency I put together a couple really cool videos right here [Music]

- stop thinking of yourself as a service company and start thinking more like a product company now I know you might be saying but Greg I own an agency or I'm a freelancer I'm a consultant and my clients pay me because I do the work for them now as a service provider your unique methodology for doing the thing that you're good at the thing that you do that is your product or at least it's going to become that if you haven't done so far now just like in the book Bill to sell which by the way I recommend you read which is actually the first real book that I've ever come across that introduced the word productization or productized services for the first time and in that book Alex the main character owns a creative agency they did all of the things they had all different sorts of clients that needed all different sorts of work they did writing ads they did logos they did websites they did brand identity they did a lot of different things they wrote TV spots things like that now they realize that they're actually best at creating logos in fact they're happiest clients came from the that they did logo creation for so much so that when they actually sat down and thought about it they realized they had a five-step process for creating and designing a logo that their clients love every single time they followed that same process that process was and became their product they stopped doing everything else and so as a service company you're ultimately a collection of people those people have specific expertise and you offer specific services in the form of Labor to a Marketplace of you know your ideal prospects now the good service companies they usually have some sort of unique approach and or a methodology along with those talented people the problem is as long as those businesses service businesses even good service businesses customize the approach to solving a client's problem for each and every new client there is actually little to no scalability to that business and its operations become really contingent on the people and when people are the main asset of the business those people can come and go overnight I don't know if you've experienced this maybe in your business or a business you've been a part of but you had a key team member leave and clients left with that person that was because the clients actually valued the person more than the company and the company's process and approach and that is a Flight Risk now don't confuse this with me saying that people are not valuable you can have a lean and mean team you can have a big team but the key pieces that the clients are buying the outcome they are buying the process that you have created that is your product it doesn't rely on any one person that any one of your clients will be like oh my God I couldn't have done it without this one person because it's a combination most likely of people that lead to a specific result working through your proprietary process here's the thing when they realize this in the book they started focusing on selling logos they got more confident in their sales process sales became easier the team that he needed to have became leaner and was able to to focus on Specialists not just a bunch of generalists and there was less operational drag inside of the business so when you start thinking of your company as a product company and focus on doing one thing and doing that thing very very well the quality of your work is going to improve you'll stand out from your competition and you'll get to the point where if you actually want to sell your business your business will be more sellable because buyers will look at it as more valuable because it's not dependent upon any one person so the question you might be asking now is how do I go from services to a product well I've talked about this a lot on this channel you can actually check out the playlist in the description below I want to share kind of the high level steps first you need to create a standard service offering that standard service offering needs a consistent process for delivering it and again ideally delivering that process is tied to an outcome or a specific result that your prospect your client will deem valuable that could pay a premium for and the last piece is really making sure that that's something that clients want right if you're solving a painful problem they'll probably pay a lot more for it if the problem is a small problem to them you probably won't be able to charge as much so depending upon who your ideal client is your process needs to solve a problem or give a result that would be valuable to your ideal client now a bonus here is that you have process that solves a problem that the client needs on somewhat of a regular basis where you can incorporate a recurring Revenue component now that might be actually a recurring Revenue stream that is tied to you know an ongoing service or it could just be more of like a replenishable thing for example in the book built to sell Alex would work with companies that would continually roll out different product lines so one client could ultimately need additional logos every year as long as they kept creating products and so they would get repeat customers and so a repeat customer is another form of recurring Revenue if that is the type of clientele that you have and so ideally again bonus you have a problem that you are solving that a lot of people need and will need over and over and over again now if you're like me and or any of our clients and you're looking for a business that is more healthy and profitable and isn't dependent upon you and you want to have a business that's built around your life one that you can actually have and enjoy looking at your agency or your service company as a product company is the first major mindset shift that you need to have before you actually start doing the work because if you can't look at your business as a product you're going to always play the game the way that you've probably been playing it is I get a couple clients I need more people on that capacity one client needs this another client needs that and the operational drag of you trying to figure out how to manage all of these people is preventing you from building a business that can give you the life that you want and Love by focusing on the thing that you are best at and that you want to work on each and every day so what I'd love to know is in the comments section join me below and I want to have you share what is the one standard service offering that you believe you should be focused on going after that you know clients need that you can do the best work for and that will ultimately become your product maybe you already have result kind of stinks and so when we focus on smaller promises we can actually get results faster getting people bought in on our methodology our process and wanting to work with us longer and so sometimes figuring out the right containers like we talked about up here you know is it a book is it a $500 a month membership is it a high ticket problem you really need to look at each one of these things let me clean this up here for a second and let's zoom in on kind of this like middle common path this was actually one of the examples that came up at our Retreat is having something low ticket into a recurring membership that is kind of like an all access pass into kind of a higher premium ticket offer that has a lot more one-on-one and so let's just zoom in on that one for example you'd be much better served if we looked at your who your what and your how that was going to happen inside this container so whether it's a $500 a month membership for 6 months or for 12 months or in perpetuity you need to ask well who is the person that is coming into this thing and what is the problem that's being solved does it require a six-month engagement does it require a 12-month engagement is the promise big enough is the work that they have to do or the work that we have to do for them with them how is our process going to take how long right that's going to dictate how big of a container we need and start to inform what the price point is going to be in order to move someone through our process and then again if we determine the who the what and the how for this one particular container inside of our offer structure then we need to look at well where are they before this point which would inform the what problem we need to solve and the how that might be happening earlier in that person's journey and on the flip side well once they've solved this problem and they've followed our process to achieve it what's the next problem that they have because that's going to inform what the next container Andor offer in our offer stack might be if you've made the mistake like I have in the past and some of our members were about to go down the path of just seeing how sexy or appealing any one of these containers could be and just executing without focusing on the person the problem and your process to solve it you could be solving a big problem in too small of a container you could be solving a problem that the wrong person has you could be trying to solve a problem with the wrong process or not giving yourself enough time to execute on the process to actually solve the problem and this is where it when done incorrectly or too quickly and or just copying someone else taking your ideas and trying to structure them into a 90-day program just because somebody else did 90 days is really what leads to the frustration the lack of client results that you might be experiencing or the frustrations you're having and why it might not be working for you so I really wanted to share this coming out of the retreat because one of our members is actually like this would be a fun topic to share on your YouTube so hopefully you got value from this and I've saved you some time and now you focus on what really matters which is your person your problem and your process and so I think hopefully you can see now that what makes a business model work isn't necessarily the containers and the order and sequence in which clients and Prospects go through said container it's having an offer that deeply resonates with the right people that you can get results following your process now seeing as how everything comes down to this offer I think you should go check out this video because I break down seven lessons that I learned after reviewing 1,000   — *greghickman: The One-Person Agency Model Saved His Life*

- in the last8 or so years we've consulted over 700 digital and creative agency owners and service providers ranging from $100,000 a year to $5 million a year and these are the five lies that are keeping them stuck and if you're new to my channel my name is Greg Hickman I'm the founder of alt agency and for the last 10 years I've been building my own business after spending the first decade of my career working in both large and boutique agencies and our agency was doing marketing automation behind the scenes of big names like Dan Martell John Dumis of entrepreneur on fire and more and over the last 7 years we've been actually training and Consulting other agency owners on how to systematize productize and scale line number one is that you cannot let go and or transition out of your existing Legacy clients I get it there's probably clients that you have that have been paying your bills and probably have been working with you for a while now maybe they've even become friends and it's really difficult to go have conversations with them about where your business is going because that future VIs might not actually be something that includes them and the reality is that we're always building two businesses the first business is the business that we have right now and the second business is the business that we want to have in the future and so if your future business has you operating and offering different things or at least more refined and productized and your current Legacy clients are not that we need to create some sort of transition plan out I know that when I personally had conversations with a handful of the clients that we knew were kind of holding us back they were very open because they were entrepreneurs as well we created a transition plan some of which were 60 days some of which were 3 months it gave us a Runway of winding down the revenue that we had been relying on while replacing that Revenue with new clients that fit the Future Vision of our business in my interview with Rob which you can check out in the description below he even shares that he had conversations with many of his clients some of which it was you know hey it's time we have to part ways and let's have a transition out which gave him a timeline that was realistic versus them just upping and leaving there were also a few clients that fit the mold of where he wanted to go they just needed to restructure how the engagement was what was included and how much they were paying and so he was able to have those candid conversations and for many of them there was a win-win and he kept more clients than he was kind of expecting even though he was changing how the service was going to be delivered so the number one lie holding many agency owners back from growth is that they don't think that they can let go of the clients that got them to where they're at now the second lie that holds most agency owners back from growing and building a business that they love is thinking that my clients always want custom Services now I get it as a traditional agency model which I came from and maybe you came from it's very easy to hold on to all of your expertise and respond to rfps or do an audit and then figure out you know client a needs this client B needs that and have every single client that comes in be unique be a bespoke solution but the reality is when you operate that way you kind of become stuck just because you're doing whatever it is the client wants back to being stuck to those Legacy clients when you don't know where the next client is coming from sometimes you'll just let the scope of work expand with those Legacy clients because you don't know where the next one's coming from and you don't want to lose the current income that you have and so naturally you became a custom solution provider for each and every client but here's the thing if you actually start selling outcome like an actual result or even a system to get a specific outcome you kind of go back into control so you get to go find clients that want that specific outcome and say hey if you have this problem I can solve it this is why productizing your service is so important what is a product it is a fixed outcome with a fixed timeline with a fixed price and when you have that you don't need to go do audits or figure out oh you need this I'll give you that you need this different thing I'll give you that you get to just go and say hey I work with this type of business that has this problem and if you productize correctly around the skill sets that you know you're really Best in Class at you will find that you're commonly solving a handful of problems that a lot of other businesses that look just like your ideal client also have and so you don't have to be custom you can actually just sell a specific outcome and especially in a market like we are in right now where there's High skepticism the buying decisions a little bit slower if you're trying to solve solve large multifaceted problems it just becomes a more difficult sale but if you say hey we solve this specific problem for this specific type of person and I can get you that outcome in this time frame the sale becomes a lot easier because the outcome is something that's more tangible your prospects can see it they can grasp it whereas a lot of full service agencies that solve a variety of problems that take 6 to 12 months to solve it's very hard for your prospects to see what getting that result will actually look like and feel like in their business so instead of going custom you can actually look at the services that you have the skill set that you have and look at the problems that it typically solves for your ideal clients and make sure that it's a high value one and package up your services to solve a specific problem that delivers a fixed outcome and watch your sales cycle change now the third lie that most agencies tell themselves is that I cannot remove myself from fulfillment as the founder now look I'll be the first to say we've all been in a spot where we say look it'll just get done faster if I do it which means we don't delegate it to a team member we don't go hire someone to help us we just take it on because I don't have the time to go train this person I don't have the time to go find and hire this person I might as well just do it myself but when it comes to growing an agency at some point you need to be able to work through people in fact if you're an agency owner and you are a consultant or service provider the business of agency is actually being able to recruit and hire talent because you're selling expertise now you're selling expertise via implementation but if you want to serve more people you need more bodies that can do the implementation thus you need to be able to transfer your expertise and your methodology to other people even if you're hiring help cuz there's going to be a ceiling and a cap on how many people you can physically serve and then the business becomes dependent upon you so if you've been saying well I can't remove myself from fulfillment we need to kind of go back and look at the last lie because many of these lies actually stack on top of each other and when you offer bespoke Solutions it's almost like you're guaranteeing that you have to be the person to do it because you're too close to the client you know exactly what they need but when you go to say hey I actually solved this specific problem my offer is productized finding talent that you can delegate certain parts of your process to become way simpler and again when your service is custom finding Talent recruiting Talent hiring Talent becomes not only costly because you need to go hire experts for things you don't know enough about but it's really difficult to train and so if you're still developing as a leader and now you're stacking on all of the burden of well now you got to figure out how to train and onboard new talent to take stuff off your plate you're adding complexity to your business often times at a stage that's way too early for where you need to be doing that when you could have simply just gotten more productized and solved fewer problems and have a specific Solution One Core offer that is your core capability because if it's your core capability it's going to be way easier to to train and know if someone's good at it if you always sell the same service over and over again don't you think it would be easier to hire people to help you when every single client moves through the exact same motion the exact same process now of course the outcome that they get the results the work the creative will be customized to that person but we can't be giving you know this client over here deliverables a this client over here deliverables B this client over here deliverable seat because that creates too much operational complexity for you to be able to go hire people now you have two full-time jobs recruiting and the actual Service delivery when we focus down on who we serve and what we sell hiring and all that stuff becomes a lot simpler the time to get things off your plate becomes simpler because you're constantly refining a process that you do over and over and over again line number four is when that you believe as the founder that you are the only person that can onboard new clients or that you have to be the point of contact for each and every new client now we had this client Chris who used to fly around the country 40 some odd times a year saying the same thing to each and every single person mainly because he thought that they needed him when he'd go and try and hire other people he would go hire experts that had their own strategic way of doing things and so the outcome wasn't exactly the same and clients weren't very happy what he had to actually do was unpack his process so that it could be taught and create a repeatable onboarding process and so once you have a road map of hey this is what happens when this is when we do this this is when we do that and you can not only walk your client through that in onboarding but also show them that they're they're not buying any one person they are buying an outcome and that outcome has a repeatable road map they will start to trust the process and understand that it's not about any one individual and it all starts with you as the leader you as the founder if you Empower your team and you let your clients know that the fastest way for them to get the result and the best way for them to get support inside of this process is via this person that you trust and have trained they will start to trust that person you you have to transfer the trust and the authority that you've developed over time with your Market to your team and reinforce that the best service the fastest service the outcome that they came to you for will happen faster and more effectively if your team runs the process so first we have to unpack what our onboarding looks like and we've done plenty of videos on onboarding so you can check out our channel on that but onboarding is actually one of the first things that you can probably delegate inside of your customer service delivery because when you sell your core capability and you sell every single client and you move them all through the same process it's really a matter of choreography and Reps getting your team to follow the exact same steps that you do now you might not realize it but inside of your head you probably do many of the same things over and over and over again and if you are still doing custom Solutions that's probably why you can't have an onboarding process again why all these lies stack on top of each other of course it's difficult to train someone on how to onboard a client when when each and every client gets something different but when each client is buying the same thing the same outcome having a choreographed systematic repeatable process that every single client can go through is not only doable but it's also one of the first things that you can delegate unlocking a ton of time and freedom for you to focus on growing the business now the fifth lie that most agencies tell themselves that keep them stuck is that they need to always be on and available for clients which usually leads to working nights and weekends we had this client Josh who would constantly take calls while trying to attend his children's soccer game and he even shared a story of how he almost missed his son's first goal ever because he was dealing with an angry client on a Saturday here's the deal you get to set the standard as the founder people are buying into you your expertise and the outcome that you can deliver so the value that the client actually gets from you is not the fact that they're going to be able to call you whenever they want now you might think that they might even say that but you could be available all you want and if you still don't deliver on the result they're going to fire you right they're going to be unhappy so what do they really value they really value on can you do what you said you were going to do now knowing that we as the leader the founder we need to set the guide rails we need to say this is how we work this is how we play the game here is how we're going to move through the process we work with our clients on developing what we call a communication cycle and this is clearly communicating to your client when how frequently on what platform for how long you guys talk about different things when do you get reports what is an emergency what deems you texting me or calling me by the way most clients should never actually even need your cell phone number at all you should have other forms of communication where they can reach out and share that there's an emergency that you allows you to have a systematic way to respond and quite frankly the reality is if you're growing a team nine times out of 10 them contacting you isn't even going to be the fastest solution it's going to be contacting your team because they're going to have the bandwidth to go support them anyway so you do not always need to be on and or available for your client and more so you can actually set the expectations of when you will be available and when you will not and how and where and when they should reach out if they need help and how and when and how frequently you will be connecting with them to make sure they are getting what they came for so those are the top five lies that are keeping most agency owners stuck from growing a business that they love and scaling to the heights that they want to get to I want to know in the comments what other lies have you want to see it I don't want you to go to the ATM machine to find out you don't have enough money to pull money out because you're not aware have the app on your phone check it often if you don't like do favors and never returning it in the first place rich people say no more than they say yes because a yes is a no to their future and a no is a yes to their goals what do you want to create in the future it's actually okay to be a little selfish to create a better scenario for who who you are and what access to resources you have so you can be richer so you can be more for people in the future which brings us to Tiny habit number three set clear goals when I started my first company that succeeded spheric Technologies I read a book called good to great and it said you have to have a vision for your life so I sat down and I created a vision 5 years five continents 50 million in Revenue 50 people I don't even know where it came from it sounded good did I get to that level in 5 years no just shy though and that was the business that made me a multi-millionaire what I had was clarity and focus see a goal properly set is half complete most people just don't write it down without a deadline that goal is just a dream so I use this thing called the rule of 300 to help me hit my goals it's all about having 100% Clarity 100% belief 100% at the time if you do that you'll hit your goal which brings us to Tiny habit Number Four review your goals daily every day I use triggers when I sit at my chair in the morning when I'm doing my morning routine or I get in my car or I come to my desk at my office to remind me to review my goals that are on my phone so it's literally pinned at the top of my notes file and they're my 12 power goals for the year most people set goals and don't review until the end of the year and they realize either hit them or didn't hit them it's like business plans they usually write them and then stuff them in a drawer I review my goals three times a day the way I make it easy on myself is I just connect it to a trigger it literally activates this thing called your reticular activating system to help you scan the world for opportunities to make that a reality it's it's like when you're thinking of buying a new car let's say a red Volkswagen and for the longest time you didn't see any other red Volkswagens all of a sudden you can't stop seeing them all over the place why your brain is on the search of red Volkswagen so you'll see them all over the road which brings us to Tiny habit number five automate your savings My Philosophy is very simple I want to set it and forget it I want to make the decision when I'm feeling good about life and then never revisit it even though I live off 10% of my income most people should follow the 50 30 20 rule which is 50% of their income goes to what they need 30% goes to what they want and the other 20% should go to savings what I do pretty much every week is any extra cash in all of my companies get swept out put into my hold Co and then my hold Co automatically invested in the portfolio I've already decided so I either do high-risk stuff in private Equity or I do lowrisk stuff in index funds in the public markets but I don't want to think about it I want to set it and forget it which brings us to Tiny habit number six avoid bad debt bad debt is high High interest debt this is Consumer Debt hard money lending I remember when I was starting off I had to buy some furniture and I thought I'd be like the cool guy and go get an apartment and finance a bunch of furniture cuz they said it would cost me nothing no interest payments for 3 years until it did and that interest payment plus the fact that it took me a few more years to pay it off cost me more than double of just buying the furniture outright at the beginning and did I need that at 20 years old no I was being silly lenders have an incentive to take advantage of you it's why most credit card companies try to to get you some kind of credit early on I'm talking college campuses it's wild you should only take on debt for things that can make you money think real estate think business Investments think investing in yourself where you can get an Roi by investing in the SM 500 not the S&P 500 it's all about short-term leverage don't make other people rich off of borrowing money from that which brings us to Tiny habit number seven exhaust the body tame the mind at 11 years old I got diagnosed with ADHD and it messed me up I was taking rolin and Aderall and all these pills that try to make me normal but I never felt normal when I was taking those pills I actually liked who I am what I had to do was figure out how to be me without the medication a big part of that is going to the gym that's why I say every day I go to the gym to work on my mind yes it has a physical value to doing that but if I don't go I can't think I can't focus I can't get things done in my day which brings us to Tiny habit number eight use your feed on social media to feed your mind the other day my son's friend came over after school and he was asking me how I deal with being distracted from things like girls and Tik Tok and I was laughing cuz I said I actually scroll Tik Tok 2 hours almost every day the difference is is what's on my feed I like to use my social media to teach myself things to microle learn to use it almost like a social University so what I do is I'll search for topics I want to learn about and then go look at those videos and leave comments on the videos that are good and then adjust my feed that's how I use my feed to feed my mind which brings us to Tiny habit number nine read 10 pages a day the other day I was at the gym and some kid asked me what's the number one habit millionaires have and the truth is out of all of them I had to pick from it's got to be reading the reason why is that nobody knows how to do the thing they're about to do the first time they do it and the best way to avoid all the pain of trying to do it through trial and error is to read books my life was built by books I've read over 1,600 books I actually did a video on it you can go search for it but the whole premise is that every philosophy idea process habits was all inspired by other people much smarter than me that have lived a big life and mastered skills and they were sharing everything they learned for 25 bucks and a 6-hour investment of my time I think it's the best trade in the world which brings us to Tiny habit number 10 wake up early a lot of people talk about getting up early but let me tell you why a lot of people sleep in because they stay up late what I do is I set an alarm to go to bed on time and that 5:00 a.m.

- most business owners build their life around their business and that's actually why many of them are unhappy miserable and have been falling out of love with their business so in today's video I'm going to show you how to flip the script and design a business that actually serves you and your own life in the upcoming year not day the way around now the best part is you actually don't have to sacrifice profit to do this in fact the businesses that we've built this way typically see 40 to 50% profit margins or more while still having the owner take Fridays off now if you're new here my name is Greg Hickman I'm the founder of alt agents and actually just finished training a bunch of our private clients and members on this exact same framework and over the last handful of years we've used it to help 800 owners build their business around their dream lifestyle one of our clients Nathan actually went from working a crazy schedule where his like friends actually had an intervention with him to dialing in his work week planning out his year so that he took time off finally enjoys weekends now and the byproduct of that was his business actually doubled while now working less and taking more time for himself I really think that this is something that can help you it's something that I've been doing in every single year since I've started this I haven't missed the family vacations I've spent more time doing the things that I love that are not business related like mountain biking spending time with my children hanging out with my friends being a part of my community and exploring other interests and hobbies that I have and so if that's something that you've been yearning to do but business has always gotten in the way it's time to follow a process to make sure that your business goes around your life now want to give a quick shout out to two people that I learned this process from originally taky Moore from million dooll coach and Dale Bowmont these are two people that I love and adore and have kind of introduced this model to me I've modified it for my own purposes we actually have two different trainings that we take our clients through one is this where it's really about kind of designing your personal life over the course of the year so that you can then build your business around it and then we do a training called the revenue road map which is like really like where does the business then fit in and how do you hit your business goals around the lifestyle that you just designed so today we are going to dive into the lifestyle piece because it's where I see a lot of agency owners fall short now the first principle that I really want to dive into that is critical for making sure that you're actually successful with this in the first place is what I call the me first principle so many of you if you're watching this probably put everybody else but you first you put your clients that are paying you the most money first you put your family first you put your children first and while that sounds good in theory it leaves you emotionally and energetically tapped it's what I'm seeing lead to a ton of burnout in order to kind of execute on this me first possibility you need to understand that I and you can only pour from a full cup from a full glass if the glass is empty you have nothing in the tank to give right but if you have a glass that's full you can give from a surplus right so I want you to have a surplus I want you to have a surplus of energy I want you to have a surplus of creativity a surplus of energy a surplus of love and affection that you can then give to everyone around you that matters the most but if you got none of that it's going to be hard for you to show up day in and day out on an empty tank I find that when I go through these exercises and these Frameworks that we actually end up in a place where no matter the week I actually am showing up more times than not my best for those that matter most now I'm human we get sick we have bad days and bad weeks but more so than not when you design your business around your life so that you are focused on the things that you love you have the schedule that you've dreamed of you show up in a whole different way that actually is making everybody else better too so that's what I mean when I say you can only give from a surplus to set the context of this one of my mentors taught me that in entrepreneurship you actually have to Define your field of play the foundational piece of this is what we call the business lifestyle return if you've ever played any sort of sports I used to play basketball and Lacrosse like you hop on the field and their lines are already painted and you know the rules of the game you were taught the rules of that Sport and so you hop on and there's no question like you know what's inbounds what's out of bounds you know what the scoreboard looks like you know how to score all those things but in business when you get started you typically just start with like a blank sheet of paper you know that has nothing on it and the beauty in that is you get to Define what success looks like you get to draw the lines on your field of play the problem is that most entrepreneurs and even myself included when I got started we skipped that part you're out there chasing some random number or random dream and you're like am I winning I'm not sure it feels like I'm winning but I actually don't like I feel like I should feel like I'm winning but I'm not so like what's going on it's because we never took the time to draw the lines on the field our field of play you got to take time to do that and so what we do with our clients is an exercise that we call the business lifestyle return it's looking at your business through the three currencies time energy and money and we do them in that order so the first lens that we look through is time and when we look through the lens of time we're really trying to ask oursel like what does our ideal week look like what does our ideal month look like what does our ideal quarter look like what does our ideal year look like what are the rules and I want you to be realistic so for example for me I want to work Mondays to Thursdays 99 to 4:00 with Wednesday actually taking off at 1: or 2 p.m.

- Remember I only want my ideal client consuming my content.
  — *agencymavericks: Stop Trying to Go Viral. It's Making You Broke*

- Build a list of 20 to 30 decision makers who are your ideal client and are showing signals that they need what you do.
  — *agencymavericks: Why You’re Not Getting Enough Clients (Even Though You’re “Doing Marketing”)*

- Your ideal clients already have an accountant, a lawyer, maybe a business coach, an AI consultant, some other service provider, B2B service provider that they're already using.
  — *agencymavericks: Why You’re Not Getting Enough Clients (Even Though You’re “Doing Marketing”)*

- Your job is to find who the accountants, the lawyers, the business coaches, the consultants are that serve your ideal client and build a referral partnership with them.

- custom projects endless revisions unpredictable income does that sound familiar That's The Daily Grind for most marketing and creative agencies and service providers today if that's you you've probably heard about productizing your service making your service more like a product but if you're a creative person or someone who has historically done a lot of customized work you probably have been racking your brain on how can I make my business producti in fact there's probably a good chance that you think it's not even possible for the type of work that you do now if you're new here my name is Greg Hickman I'm the founder of alt agency and we've trained and consulted over 700 different marketing and creative agency owners and service providers ranging from things as unique as dream home build outs to Brand identity and brand messaging to marketing funnels Leen agencies to strategy and having seen such a wide variety of services specifically ones that thrive off creative deliverables and customizing tailored solutions to clients a lot of people start to think maybe like yourself that it's not even possible for you and so I want to share just a simple way I think a better way to think about productizing your service so that you can be more predictable more scalable meaning that you can serve more clients with less effort and that you can have a business that doesn't revolve around you the founder doing every single piece of the business now historically when people think about productized Services they think of companies like design pickle where it's a flat monthly rate and you get unlimited requests this concept of unlimited Services has become very common in especially inside of the creative side of agencies and service providers you have people like Brett over at design Joy who is a oneperson agency that makes anywhere from six to I think 10K per client and he does all sorts of creative deliverables that is one version of a productize service just like design pickle is a version of a productize service where it's a flat rate of 300 to 1,000 bucks a month you get a dedicated designer but they do only certain types of design they do a lot of different things and often times in a product High service you actually don't do a lot of different things you actually sell one particular outcome in fact the first time that I at least personally ever heard of the word producti service is in the book built to sell by John waro and the whole book is about a creative firm that specializes down into logo design that agency goes from doing all of the creative things you know advertisements Billboards brochures logos websites to only offering logos and in the story Alex the agency owner tells his mentor Ted that they have a five-step process that they pretty much always follow to create the best logos that their clients love now obviously every single client wasn't getting the same logo they got customized solutions they got customized logos and identities for their business and and their product but Alex's agency always followed a particular process a five-step process they called it the five-star logo process and every single client moved through that exact same process to get their logo from idea to delivered with Alex's agency and so with that being said I want to talk about how you can think about your process now Jack butcher the creator of visualized value on Instagram you should check him out he drew this graph at one point that I thought really just dumb down prototype service and makes it really easy to understand so I'm going to share it with you instead of thinking about productize Services as kind of this black Andor white solution it has to be unlimited services or this one specific thing I want you to start to think about prti Services as a Continuum so on one side of the Continuum you have service on the other side of the Continuum you have products in the middle you have producti Services now Jack butcher says service is work without a process many of you are probably familiar with with that client a comes in you do things one way client B comes in you do things another way so Services is work without a process if we come to the middle of this Continuum producti services are work with a process just like Alex in built to sell his agency always followed the exact same fstep process to serve their clients and give them an amazing experience that resulted in a logo that they loved and on the opposite side of the spectrum you have products like physical products so imagine walking down the aisle of the grocery store and you see a box of cereal right there is a picture of the cereal on the front that is what you are going to get on the side of the Box are the nutritional ingredients here's what's included the ratios the percentages what it's made of on the back there's probably a little bit of a story on how this came to be where it was made all that great stuff and you have this very specific thing right and with the example of cereal you have the bowl on the front you know with the classic milk pouring in and obviously the milk and the bowl sometimes even the strawberries aren't actually even included in the thing but the Box makes it clear as to what you getting you are getting a specific physical product that you can touch and hold it doesn't change in real time as you pick it up off the Box the scope of that cereal cannot change so on one side you have service again which is work without a process productize service which is work with a process then you have product which is process without work think about software solutions that do things for you AO SAS companies like that's product right they do something and there's not a lot of Labor tied to it once the code has been created so the question that you really need to ask yourself when it comes to your particular type of service your particular type of deliverables is how product ties do you want to be we have clients that have basically wanted to not be involved in client fulfillment ever and not really deal with clients which means we need to think about how can we get their clients the result as close to a product as possible Right right their way towards the product side of the Continuum then you have some service providers and clients that we've worked with that genuinely enjoy being a part of the client experience they don't necessarily care as much about scalability but they want to enjoy all of the work that they do and you find people that are then towards the middle or the left side of the Continuum that are a little bit more service oriented and so the real question is how produ tize do you want to be how much customization do you want to offer how much standardization do you want to offer how systematic should it be should it be like a product where there is little to no levels of fulfillment or do you want it to be closer to the service side where you meet with your clients and things like that where on that sliding scale do you want your service to be and as you look at where you want to be on this Continuum of how productized would you like your company to be what are the appropriate ingredients and methodologies and approaches that you can deploy to make sure that you have leverage that you have efficiency and that you can deliver with the minimum amount of view as possible while also maintaining client results that means the way that you deliver your creative service might look actually different from another creative service provider that offers the exact same thing but has a different goal and different values as it comes to how productized they want to be and now after having consulted over 700 different agencies doing this I firmly believe that you have to start thinking about this as a Continuum because there is no black and white it's amazing that we live in a world today where you have someone like Brett at design Joy who built it around himself and he doesn't at least currently want to have a lot of people that work for him that creates a certain type of Lifestyle for him I know other people that would hate that and that's why it's a Continuum you get to design it how you want to design it but at the end of the day we're either looking at something that is service which is work without a process or on the far side product which is process without work Andor somewhere in the middle where it's work with a process each and every one of you you no matter if you're a marketing agency or a creative service provider of some sort likely has a process and that is exactly where you need to start and once you've identified your process that's where you can start to systematize that process so if you want to learn how to systematize your process and exactly what order you should think about systematizing your business go check out this video here

are two ways to get leads one is you can get paid traffic and the other way you can do it is organic traffic which I'll talk about in a moment now once you've got those leads you need to convert them into clients and that's all about having the right message or the right offer to the right people at the right time right once you've converted a client obviously you need some services to sell them and the idea with services is that you need to know your sweet spot that is the things that you do really well and that you're really passionate about and that really excite you ideally you should specialize in solving a very specific problem for a very specific type of client and then ultimately what you want to do is productize those services into something that you can sell over and over again on recurring revenue which I'll talk more about the moment as well finally now behind the scenes what you need is some processes and really processes are going to help you stay consistent and deliver consistent outcomes to your clients and run the business in a consistent way and also the processes are going to make you more efficient and an efficient business is how you make profit and really the three big processes that you need is a sales process that is a way of getting leads and converting them into clients and making revenue you need an Operations process in other words a way to actually deliver these services to your clients and you need a financial process to make sure that you're profitable and then finally at some point whether it's now or in the future you are going to need some people to actually help you in the business because people are what give you leverage in other words let's do something once teach someone else how to do it so they can do it so then you can multiply it people are also would allow you to scale and ultimately how you make a profit because without people you just basically selling time for money and you're gonna get overwhelmed so they are the ingredients for a successful business now what I want to do is walk you through how the ultimate agency bundle helps you implement all of that in your business all right so how do we get leads there's two ways you can get leads you can pay for traffic which really at the moment is all about Facebook Ads okay so that's where the Facebook ads accelerator course is going to help you figure out how to use Facebook ads to get leads to your website organically you're going to need to use content to get leads from from the Internet to your website for free right oh we're using organic traffic methods you know search and social sharing and so our content strategy course is going to help you design a content strategy to attract the right person at the right time and put the right message in front of them I'm going to come back to these in a moment to in a moment in order to get those leads and turn them into clients you need the right message to right people at the right time this is where the blueprint course is really going to help you understand how to craft your unique value proposition so that you are solving a very specific problem to a very specific type of person and our high ticket sales funnel of course is actually going to give you the mechanics of how to set that up how to take a lead through a sales funnel and convert them into a high paying client now in terms of services this is this is what I love about the ultimate agency bundle these three courses here the Facebook Ads course the content strategy blueprint course and the high ticket sales funnel course are all services that you can now content strategy blueprint and a high ticket sales funnel they are all services that you can now offer clients so they're things that you can use in your own business to help grow your own business and they're now services that you can offer and the blueprint course is really going to help you figure out your sweet spot and how to specialize in that and then ultimately how to productize that that service into recurring revenue from a processors point of view the blueprint is going to really help you get all of your processes sorted out from attracting leads converting them into clients delivering great project getting referrals following up with clients onboarding off-boarding all of the processes in the blueprint are going to help you run your business in a consistent and efficient way and with and we're also we also touch on hiring people in the blueprint but we've got a dedicated hiring a team master class which is going to walk you through how to hire people how to onboard them how to train them now this is just the tip of the iceberg a couple of other things that we've got is the lead gen master class we a deep dive into how to craft your offer regardless of whether using Facebook ads or content so this is a really powerful masterclass in lead gen we've also got the recurring revenue master class down here which is really going to help you understand how to productize your services and we've also got a whole bunch of other bonuses like the mini chat messenger bot master class now a mini chat is a great way to get free traffic from facebook using facebook Messenger get those people into your funnel have a conversation with them and turn them into clients again I'm just touching on the tip of the iceberg key if you have a look at everything included in the ultimate agency bundle you'll see how everything we've got in that bundle fits into each part of this this recipe if you like and helps you take these ingredients and install them in your business

- these are the best businesses you can start in 2024 Warren Buffett says great businesses have high gross margin so what is gross margin essentially if I sell a product for $5 but it cost me $1 to make then my gross margin is $4 or 80% see the best businesses are ones where you have the highest margin and then also sell for the highest dollar amount what you don't want to be is like a restaurant where the gross margins are only 45% you want to be on the upper end of 70 80 even 90% % gross margin and to do this you need to understand all the industries that's why I've spent a lot of time analyzing all the best Industries and evaluating all the gross margins from lowest to highest that you can start today with very little experience to make this year the best year of your life the first business is a product business typically these companies have 50 or 60% gross margin now I've been involved in some incredible product companies like paila that does a biodegradable phone case or laundry sauce which is a modern-day detergent company or Lomi that does a food composting device and I mean these are literally some of the best companies out there but I've seen people lose their shirt Building Product companies because they don't understand how to get distribution how to get sales how to build a product efficiently and essentially they take all their savings put it into a product don't get any Revenue so if you want to make a product business work great these are the three things that you need to do right number one is you have to pre-sell see most product companies use Kickstarter or Indiegogo or other crowdfunding platforms so that they can validate demand for the product without losing money now I know you've probably been burned by investing in some of these crowdfunding campaigns but the best ones know how to do them promote them Market them get the money and then use that to actually build the product the second thing is you have to do direct to Consumer and the reason why is you got to skip the retailers because if you go through a wholesaler to a retailer by the time you actually get paid and there's the big cash flow issues it's very hard to make a product business work that's why Amazon has been a big category for a lot of people that are getting into the product space and the third thing is you have to invest in the Brand This Means making sure that you use premium products meaning that you care about Word of Mouth that you focus on reviews that you look for opportunities to get pressed to drive traffic to the business because that's what's going to create the flywheel to make a product business work the second business is an agency or sometimes a service business business the margins are between 60 and 70% now I've learned over the years that the easiest companies are ones where you take a skill that you already have maybe as an employee and then offer that skill to other businesses and get paid to either do for those businesses what you currently do or teach them how to do it for themselves now I've seen a lot of people launch different types of agencies social media marketing agencies Etc and the ones that actually scale and make money not just have a low paying full-time sometimes dou double full-time job they focus on these four things number one is they focus on a strong pain in the market if you think about it you could start an AI company today where you go and you analyze businesses and all the inefficiencies and then introduce them the AI tools to solve those problems and you would make a ton of money because businesses will pay for anybody that can show them how to save them or make them money using AI is a modern-day way of them getting leverage and you can even negotiate where you get paid more based on how much money or time you save them number two is productize Services see if you sell just work for time then you're always going to get paid for your hour what you want to do is get paid for an outcome and to do that you got to focus on one thing if you're a designer focus on Logo designs if you're a programmer focus on the onboarding experience if you're a salesperson focus on the Discovery call teach people how to do one specific thing so that you can productize it and as you grow you can buy back back your time by having somebody else support different aspects of that productize service number three is focus on reoccurring Revenue see too often when we start we just want to like get paid to do the thing problem is that every month you got to get paid to do the thing over and over and over again so you start at zero at the beginning of every month what you want to do is sell a service an ongoing reoccurring Revenue Service for whatever that work is so that software work because if you don't have people using and loving it the way you need to then it's not going to create the margins cuz customers are going to come and they're going to go number one is a sticky product you want to build a tool that solves a problem in a business where they have to use it every day think about things that are painkillers not vitamins you want to create something that's identity and that's why we see a lot of our clients that come in and they don't clearly get a picture of where they're going and it's so much easier to Resort back to the traditional way of operating see a lot of tactical claims and things like that if you scroll through any sort of social media you know how to get 100 appointments or we'll help you get 100 appointments and yada yada with four seconds of work and you do nothing and all these you know bold claims but you see a lot of those similar claims from a lot of similar people and at some point and we're already seeing this is if you say the same thing as everybody else the how how you get there is actually going to be pretty important so your way of doing things your way of work your approach is actually really important I think comes second to that identity right there's going to be a lot of people that want your mechanism wants your pathway but they actually care about a different outcome that you might disagree with those are actually not qualified prospects so when we find someone in our world that is kind of the scalar die mentality and all they care about is money and arguably they're not even married or have kids or care about any of those things like they're really not our people because our business the ethos and the beliefs that all agency owners have is to build their business around their life and that and things like that and so if we find someone that's like hey I love the concept of how you talk about productization I want to do that and it's for all of the wrong reasons that we disagree with - are you ready to stop the guessing game and identify prospects that are the most likely to become clients stick around because I'm going to identify the five traits of sales qualified prospects that you need to know to get more of the right clients now the big problem that I see most agencies and Consultants struggle with when it comes to sales is that they think they are talking to the right prospects and that person ends up not hiring them not closing then the agency owner thinks that they suck or that there's something wrong with their service and they go into this vicious spiral of trying to change all these things because this person said no now if you're new here my name is Greg Hickman and I run a company called all agency and we've trained over 600 agency owners and Freelancers and service providers to date on how to grow and systematize their business and after having conversations with so many different agencies what I've learned is in more cases than not we're actually just talking to the wrong person now the challenge here is being able to spot these subtle character traits these five character traits that actually lead to a prospect becoming a client or not now I've learned of these five traits from Dean Jackson and after thousands of sales calls it's way easier to spot them in fact just yesterday I was on a sales call well I was speaking to two different prospects on paper they looked identical they were within $2,000 from each other in monthly recurring Revenue they offered almost identical Services they've both been in business for about the exact same amount of time and they had the exact same challenges now one of them hired us and one of them did not and the one that did not did not have these five character traits and I'm going to break down each one and hopefully help you realize that sometimes when you don't get the deal you just didn't realize that you were talking to the wrong person so what are the five character traits that make up the most sales qualified prospects for your agency character trait number one are they willing to engage in a dialogue now if they booked a call on your calendar from a referral or from a piece of content or from you know you got into the conversation in the DMS and they booked the call and they were engaging with you then they probably checked this box meaning they're willing to engage in a dialogue that's the first step some of you guys run into issues where you're chasing this person and they're avoiding you which means they haven't gotten this one trait of willing to engage in a dialogue which brings us to the second trait are they friendly and collaborative you probably have had a situation where you've tried to get more information from the prospect that you were speaking to in order to help them them and diagnose if you actually could help them and they were unwilling to give you the information they were unwilling to collaborate for example we helped our clients grow their revenue and there have been instances where I've asked okay well I'm trying to understand where you're on business where are you at in Revenue right now and where do you want to get to and these people were unwilling to share this information as if it were so proprietary and important and guess what me understanding where you're at in revenue and what your profit profit margins look like and what you pay yourself actually impact how I would prescribe to fix that problem because I need to know in context where you're at in your business and and what systems you have in place and do you have the funds to invest in certain things and if the person that you're speaking to isn't friendly and not collaborative in that they're not even willing to answer the questions that you are asking that will lead you to be able to with confidence know that you can help them or not they are not a qualified Prospect I don't care if on paper they have enough money if they match all the other character traits if they cannot collaborate with you to determine if it's a good fit they're not qualified end of story number three is do they know what they want so for many of you the show stops right here I've had plenty of conversations where we hop on a call someone saw an ad that we ran about you know helping people and helping agencies grow and productize their service and when they get on and we're like What would success look like what do you want to have happen if we were to work together how would we measure success and they literally have no idea believe me like you can ask tons of questions you can probe there are some people that just don't know what they actually want and they're having conversations like the one they have with you with tons and tons of people in hopes of you telling them what they should want the reality is if your prospect needs you to tell them what they want that is going to be a horrible client to work with because now they need you to know what they want that is not a great place to be in so do they have these first three traits are they willing to engage in a dialogue are they friendly and collaborative do they know what they want which brings us to the fourth trait do they know when they want it there are some people that have problems they're friendly collaborative they know what they want but they have other priorities going on and they don't know exactly when they want this outcome or when they want help now you can obviously ask questions and help them figure this out and prioritize there's definitely some sales technique here I don't want to overlook the the sales tactics but I also think that in our world today in agency sales online sales that we rely too heavily on the tactics leads many people to selling the wrong person out of a pressure-based sales conversation or trying to convince someone that they have a problem and that they need help with it all of those will come around to bite you in the butt those are the clients that will churn out those are the clients that won't give you a testimonial those are the clients that are going to be the biggest headache and all because you had to tell them they needed to solve this thing right now and they on their own will did not decide when they actually want to solve this problem because in the back of their head they have other top priorities that are above this and if you can't help them figure out why your solution your service should be a higher priority cuz they don't believe it that's going to be a difficult person to work with no matter how you slice and dice it so that brings us to number five do they want you to help them in this day and age there's a lot of experts and there's a lot of agencies out there and in previous videos I've talked talked about why it's important to be able to demonstrate your expertise Sometimes the best way to help a prospect realize that they need your help is to actually consult them you know the old trick of helping them to demonstrate that you can help them so that they want more help right so do they actually want you to help them and in the case of my calls yesterday we had this one Prospect who's got on the call again these people looked identical on paper one was like look I've watched your testimonials I've watched hours of videos on YouTube you're the only person is talking about these problems like I've been thinking about them I need help and I want you to help me because I can see that you've helped a lot of people they're literally admitting that they want me specifically and my company to help them that person becomes a client on the other hand the other person they want to be the one that can do it on their own like so desperately they won't admit it to themselves but all of their behaviors actually will show you that they don't want you to help them they wanted you to help diagnose the problem and help them get clear on what to do so that they could do it themselves so they might have been willing to engage in a dialogue they might have been friendly and collaborative they might have known what they wanted and they might have known when they want it but they didn't want you to help them they want to do it themsel sometimes you just have the person that is this way it's baffling I know and again there are some sales trainers out there that will teach you ways to maneuver this and make this person think that they actually want you to help them I've tried those tactics my experience is if they don't want you to help them at some point that will become realized for them inside of your delivery experience and they'll walk they'll be The Annoying client they'll be the Pia client they'll complain and it'll be a miserable experience and it won't be worth your time you probably can think of a name right now of someone that you enrolled that you just convinced that they wanted you to help them if they don't want you to help they're not qualified so they can check all four of the five boxes and they're still not qualified and if you're sitting there beating yourself up that that person didn't close or hire you that's going to impact you talking to the next qualified Prospect and probably self-sabotage that call so again after working with 600 different people time and time again I see in more cases than not we actually thinking the prospect is qualified when they're missing one of these five traits IND or a combination of these five traits they need to have all five are they willing to engage in a dialogue are they friendly and collaborative do they know what they want do they know when they want it and do they want you to help them that is what identifies for you a sales qualified prospect that will become a great client I hope you found this video useful I know it might be a little bit different than what some other folks out there say but in my experience the best clients are the ones that want you to help them and meet these five criteria go ahead and hit that like button and that notification Bell because we have videos like this each and every week and we'll see you in the next one
