- becoming an entrepreneur and going into business for yourself especially as an agency owner or service provider if you didn't pay attention to the fine print when you decided to go out on your own you are going to be in big trouble now maybe this is a realization that you're coming to right now as you watch this video but here's a few things that you need to understand when you decided to leave your nine to five and go into business for yourself because you knew that there was an opportunity to have an uncapped ceiling on your income and to control your calendar you know get the freedom that you wanted that you weren't getting out of working at your former agency or your former job there are things that you need to know come along with that decision and there's three of them that I think many people Overlook and if you overlook them and decide not to follow them growing your own business to the point where you have that freedom and you have the income that you actually feel like is what you're worth and more than what you could have made at a job it's going to be difficult to get there fine print Point number one you are a marketer if you go into business for yourself you have to understand that you have have to go out there and Market yourself to get business you are a marketer you are a salesperson it is your job to help enroll people into your vision about to how your service can actually solve their problem if you're not willing to go out into the marketplace especially today in this Creator economy and share your point of view and add value back into the world via your free content if you're not willing to do that in order to get the right fit clients building a business is going to be really difficult now you might say Greg but I could go hire someone to be my marketer and while that is true in the early stages of trying to get traction if you're gonna try to go find someone that is going to do all the heavy lifting to make awareness to get clients and then close them marketing and sales I think you're going to be really challenged to find someone that's willing to do that for you when they could easily go do it for themselves because they don't need you and so if you are really the technician and you only want to be inside of fulfillment you're probably going to need to find that person now good luck finding them they are out there but if you're less than seven figures and you haven't really created any sort of product Market fit I think in my experience it's going to be difficult to find that person so if you're going out on your own you have to be willing to Market yourself acquire the skills of becoming a marketer and a salesperson because without you knowing it you choosing to become an entrepreneur and owning your own agency you also decided to become a marketer and a salesperson fine print number two is that you also signed up for the long game the number of people that we've worked with and talked to that want this Silver Bullet where they go from 10K a month to 100K a month sustainable without stress and in a way that like there's no obstacles and hurdles is so high that I think people believe that entrepreneurship is just so easy if it was so easy why are less than four percent three percent depending upon the data that you look at of entrepreneurs at least in the United States only ever able to hit a million a year this is small fraction of businesses that actually do seven figures and yet you want to do that in less than 90 days you're looking for the silver bullet and have it be a layup now while there are exceptions to the rule and we have seen people Skyrocket to success most entrepreneurs are actually playing the long game like you've decided to go into business for yourself to run a successful sustainable business that lets you live your life on your own terms like if you're 30 or if you're like me in your 40s you got a lot of life left to live and so you have the business and it's going to run for a while so if you think that you're building a business to be done with it in 90 days or even 12 months I think you've overlooked the entire reason to go into business for yourself in my opinion recap fine print number one you're a marketer and a salesperson find print number two you're in it for the long game and you signed up for it by becoming a business owner and number three which I actually think is the more common things is talked about at least in The Mastermind circles that I'm in is that you also signed up for the best professional and self-development program that ever existed whether you went to college or a degree or MBA whatever you did going into business for yourself it's unlike anything else that you'll ever experience you will be challenged with the highs the lows emotional mental physical you will be tested in all of these areas and it will force you to become a better person you will be more self-aware you'll be a better thinker you'll be able to challenge yourself to do bigger things because that's what's going to be required of you just by running a business just by trying to grow something that you are in full control of without even knowing that you signed up for a super intense self-development program you did when you decided to go into business for yourself so if you're an agency owner and you jumped into the game because you know that you have the opportunity to build something that gives you a life on your own terms you have to understand that you have to go out there and market and sell yourself to get that product Market fit and earn the right of becoming a CEO because if you look at what the role of a CEO is the role of a CEO is Vision people and cash so if you ain't got no people and you ain't got no cash that's your job as the founder to to start building that up so that you can have the vision to enroll people into it both clients and team members to play this long game of a business I bring all of this up because I had a couple conversations with some past clients around they don't want to be sales people they don't want to be marketers inside of their business they'd rather delegate that but they're still at such an early stage of their business that they're barely clearly articulating what it is they do they have to get better at that first before they can get someone else to sell on their behalf if you can't sell your service and get someone else to give you money to solve their problem why do you think some other person is going to be able to come in there and do that if you can't even clearly articulate it you're going to waste time trying to train that person when you should have done it yourself that doesn't mean you have to do sales forever but you have to get good enough at some of these things to be able to one know if the person you're hiring is going to be good at it and two get them up to speed fast enough where it's cost effective to actually be bringing this person on if you're out there and you went into business for yourself just know that you got to pay attention to the fine print you're salesperson you're in it for the long game and it's the best professional and self-development course program education that you're ever going to get

- in this video I want to share the five stages that you're going to move through in order to remove yourself from the client service delivery inside of your agency or your consultancy when I got started in my own agency we were building out sales and marketing funnels for online course creators authors speakers and coaches and they came to us and they said look I've been selling this stuff you know doing live launches live webinars and I want to make this more automatic and so we were able to help them build an automatic lead generation system a way to capture their leads a sales engine and then a delivery system that onboarded their clients with kind of predictability and consistency when we started working with agencies and consultants and they're like we want this automatic sales system just like you've been doing I asked well what do you sell and they said well what do you mean it's whatever the client needs and I was like oh that's why you don't have any sort of system or automated workflows it's because each and every client requires something different and that's the key piece here in order to get leverage so first and foremost thing that we need to do is to productize our service and we've talked about this before on this channel but right now you probably find yourself on the custom Services side which means on this level of Leverage you are a high labor low leverage each client needs something different on the complete other side of the Spectrum which might sound super desirable is like a product this could be a digital course which you see a lot of people selling and you might envy that and want that and on that side of the spectrum you actually have a high leverage deliverable that requires low labor and that might be the dream but in order to get there as a service provider you're going to find this sweet spot right here somewhere on this spectrum is a productized service and a productized service is that single service that yields a specific outcome for a specific person your ideal client that solves a problem that they have and value it's a problem that you want to solve you're good at solving that they will pay a premium for and you can solve it over and over and over again so the key here is get rid of this notion of custom and each and every client gets something different I know that goes counter to what some people teach but you need to unlearn this tradition agency model and in order to find leverage we want to actually start making our service more like a product hence productize your service and when we do that you're going to go from where you're currently at inside the leverage ladder which is one-on-one it's custom services and maybe it's just you maybe you are the bottleneck inside of your delivery what we want to do is if that's you we want to move up to at least level two which might still be one on one but now we are actually selling a productized offer so that single service that yields a specific outcome and it's still you but what you'll notice is when you make this transition from Level 1 to level two inside the leverage ladder you're going to be able to serve more clients somewhere between two to four times the number of clients that you're working with right now just yourself if you model it correctly from there you might say I want more leverage right this is where you go one-on-one it's still productized another term for this if your brain is not comprehending is systemized or maybe programmatic and this is whereas you plus a team so when we were building webinar funnels for our clients at first it was just me doing custom webinar funnels then we realized hey there's a specific way in which this works so I moved up to level two which gave me a little bit more leverage I was able to work with more clients actively and the juggling act from two to three clients to five to ten clients myself became more manageable then I brought in a team and were able to increase that capacity again we still worked with clients one-on-one but each and every team member allowed us to serve more clients and so we went from you know one to one into one to productize then we made level four where we actually went from one on one to one to many and this is where the productized service started to become more like a program we started packaging up our templates and our intellectual property and again this was you aka me with a team you might want to always be involved with your client delivery I know that I really enjoy working with clients so I don't have a problem being involved in certain parts of the process now the business can run without me being there clients can still get results but I'm not the sole bottleneck in inside of our clients getting the results so you and team that might be the highest form that you want but there is another form where it's one to many and this is where I'll actually use the word program and it's delivered entirely by your team meaning you don't need to be involved at all like I said you may not want to go to level five or be interested in that right now but what I've found to be true is if you're currently down here in level one there are specific steps that you need to take in order to start moving up no one just jumps from level one to level five right you're gonna probably evolve over time get really good at going from Custom to committing to the one thing that you're really good at and you move to level two and you're gonna spend some time there you're going to be getting clients results you're going to be collecting more money and you're going to say hey I actually have money to go hire people start bringing in my team I'm going to start having people handle onboarding instead of me which allows me to you know serve my clients better focus on other parts of my business then we move up again to level four and so on and so forth so you want to be able to start positioning yourself to move up this ladder of Leverage inside of how you do deliver your results to your clients like I said in the beginning it really all starts with looking at your service more like a product meaning that it solves a specific problem for your ideal client and you go find more people that have that problem and you sell and deliver that solution over and over and over again and I don't know about you but if you've ever done something a lot you get better at it you get faster and you get more efficient all of those things actually will equal for you more profit less headache more predictability and a business that actually will run without you so if you're an agency owner or a consultant expert freelancer and you're caught up in this agency trap this traditional agency trap of full service I gotta do custom everything if a client says jump you say how high and you're really just a glorified employee and you really want to make a change so that you can have a business that serves you and have the lifestyle that you want then this is a pathway that's going to be extremely helpful and you need to focus on moving up that leverage ladder because the more you move up that leverage ladder the the more capacity you can handle so going to get five clients to 10 clients a month won't actually break you because if you're like me when I was where many of you are at right now if I got five clients tomorrow like I would completely break so it wasn't about just getting more leads it was about how can I actually get more leads but also serve them without just working more hours and working harder that's not the game the game is how do we make this simpler for us by having one standardized simple solution that solves our ideal clients problem and we get really good at mastering doing that over and over and over again that's how you're going to remove yourself from Client Service delivery so you can focus on sales marketing growing your business taking it to the next level or go mountain biking spend more time on the hobbies that you love or with your family that's what I'm doing right now in this season of my life maybe that's what you're looking for but if you want to get out and have more freedom this is the pathway and I put together one more video on more about prioritization if you want to learn more about that and you can check that out here and or in the link in the comments
  — *greghickman: Remove Yourself From Client Delivery (5 Steps)*

- working in a creative environment can be really stressful you work on different projects at different times and nothing is really planned in advance or maybe you've planned everything but a client calls you and suddenly everything just goes to sh so you end up working late or on the weekend to get everything done which means you spend less time with your friends and family but if this sounds familiar not to worry there's a very simple way to be able to fix this I'm going to walk you through a simple productivity hack for creatives that will help you take back control and win a full 8 hours a week and in order to best explain this hack I need to start by explaining a law so this is a law principle rather than a law that's going to get you in trouble the law is called Parkinson's law the concept is that humans will utilize all available resources that are made available to us from bank accounts to Earthly resources Etc but this applies primarily to time in this instance so I want to give you two different scenarios the exact same end result but two different people with two different motivations and different amounts of time available so first we have Grandma she needs to send out a birthday card to her granddaughter Grandma sits has her coffee thinks about what she wants to write in that birthday card and then she goes to CVS buys that birthday card then she goes back home thinks about exactly again what she wants to write writes it in the card and then drives to the post office to get it mailed out it took her over 4 hours to get this entirely done from beginning to end high-powered CEO she has a whole lot less time available but needs to send a birthday card to her niece she's got 30 seconds in between two meetings to get this birthday card out so she goes to her EA and says hey I need you to go down to CVS pick out a birthday card that's largely around this area and here's what I want you to write inside of it then I want you to mail it out to my niece she got the exact same thing done but in 30 seconds that took Grandma 4 hours let's take a look at how this plays out in your real life you have your day planned you have a project that you were about to start you get going on the project and then an email comes up Bing you get pulled into that email you're starting to respond in the midst of you getting that email Bing over here you now have a slack message and then finally okay I'm now back to the project again I can get back into it and then someone turns around and asks you a question and interrupts you in the midst of you doing that your phone goes Bing and you get a text message and so now you're pulled over there and this happens day after day ongoing for the vast majority of creatives there was a study that was done that dissected the actual productivity of white collar workers and found that 6 hours of an 8-hour day was wasted in these exact types of distractions and you need to avoid them so now that we have what the issue is how do we actually solve for it the first thing that is incredibly important is understand what your ideal day looks like so understanding when you're going to spend time for meetings when you're going to reply to slack messages when you're going to reply to those text messages when you have phone calls what time you're going to work on projects and when are you going to get those administrative things done understanding exactly what 30 minute blocks are dedicated for what giving you a mechanism to understand where things will fit in then we have to evaluate that long to-do list that we haven't been able to get to because of all of the things that has been creating distraction so I want you to take that to-do list and I want you to take a white sheet of paper write them all down once you have them written down we need to filter through them the first thing is am I the only person in the world to get this done should I actually be doing this is this worth my time yes or no the next thing is is this a priority it is important that we focused on the priority items that we need to get done both for your clients as well as for your agency making sure that you're able to make progress there's nothing more to feeding than having a to-do list that continues to grow that somehow ends up longer at the end of the day than how it was when you started so from that point forward this is now Friday or Thursday whenever you enjoy week you're going to go through this entire list and you're going to plan out the entire week ahead of time listing out exactly when you're going to be responding to emails and communication when you're going to work on those projects that you had on the to-do list when you're going to be meeting with both clients and your internal team being very clear of when you are going to do what I want to point out here is that you want to be careful to understand that this is not going to work perfectly well the first time you'll continue to get better over time on average this saves the vast majority of individual 8 hours a week but some people are able to save upwards of 24 hours in a week three full days of work by doing exactly this this is though just applying it to one individual let's imagine this larger extreme example I had a client that was doing about 8 million a year that 30 35 employees at the time and their team was incredibly distractable going through a Time study and looking at some screen capture softwares of what was going on within the agency Parkinson's law was happening for them there was $1.2 million of billable hours that was being wasted distracted the team was incredibly overwhelmed incredibly stressed out they were writing up a ton of time and able to save that billable Time by installing exactly this into the agency so imagine if this is the life for you and you have a team the amount of far-reaching repercussions that this has on your business and one of the most impactful pieces that it also allows for you to make progress being able to work on those things that you never were able to have time for before being able to work on your business and not just in it so now you know how to manage your day better but that doesn't change the amount of work that you have to do so check out this video where I explain the number one hack for creatives so you can spend less time working on the boring tasks and more time working on the stuff you love
  — *creativeagencysuccess: The #1 Productivity Hack for Creative Agencies*

- hey agency owners um so i have to rent with you guys for a minute i had an agency talk to me the other day about the fact that they felt like their team was not being loyal they had lost a number of team members that were really vital to the operation and the agency owner i think was rightfully upset the piece that i found to be very interesting though that i think is the crux of the problem and where he was really missing the issue and the point that i was trying to make and hopefully over time will understand a little bit more is that i asked him why he thought that that team should be more loyal and should have stayed or given more notice when they were actually leaving he's like well i have held them through the pandemic i have overpaid them i have he had all this like list and litany of reasons and i asked them well what about them on a personal level do you know them do you have any connection to them any of those pieces that would allow for someone to actually have loyalty and his answer was no and i thought that this was very interesting because i see this happen a lot where you have an organization that is completely disjointed from the people that work at the organization and what and i know that i say this all the time in sales and in management but you the thing that is missed here is that they're human beings your team members are human beings and human beings like talking about their personal life they like talking about themselves they like learning about people they like connecting with people we are herd animals and we need to actually recognize that on the base physiology and psychology of us as a species that is extremely important to actually connect with the individuals if you're actually wanting to create loyalty and so that is what my rent is about today is connect with your team members get to know them want to understand them know what they did this past weekend what do they enjoy as their hobbies what do they do in their off time why do they care about it why does that make them excited are they married are they not do they have any kids what is going on in their personal life every single one of my team members i know on a personal level and i know what's going on with their lives i know that the types of issues that they have i mean i talk to them on on a fairly regular basis to just connect with them get to know them and recognize that they're human beings and actually take an interest in them and they know that i care about them on a personal level they know that i want to help them and they know that i'm there for them should they ever need it and that i i'm very careful to make sure that i don't blur the line and i try to and sometimes maybe i do a little bit but trying to really wash that line between professionalism and friendship and make sure that you have that line really defined so that you're able to sort of walk up to the line but not exactly cross it and making sure that you're really just taking an interest in your team members that i know that there's so much that has actually been fed into organizations as like this place of culture where you're trying to just force it into the organization when it isn't something that already just innately exists and you can't just build it in so what i see happen is like at the beginning of the meeting you're going to give me your like best best business or best personal thing or you're going to tell me a little bit about what just happened last weekend and it's like this thing that just gets fit into a meeting and it's not something that people are actually interested in so it's like this obligatory thing that they have to share rather than actually wanting to engage in conversation so i don't have any of those like requirements that you have to share with someone the thing that i think that is important is that just ask a question you know change it every time it's not the exact same question it doesn't have to be this like built-in forced piece of culture that you're trying to push into your business if you don't know your team members and you have churn you haven't taken an interest in who they are you haven't connected with them i want to remind you that they are not going to be loyal this is not indentured servitude you need to actually treat them like human beings if you haven't asked a question go right now stop this video and go ask your nearest team member something about themselves
